title: C# Software Engineer – Platform Development
description: **Sustainability that means business** We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated Software Engineer to join our Platform Engineering group. In this role, you will play a key part in designing, building, and continuously improving the foundational platforms, tooling, and automation that enable our product engineering teams to deliver software efficiently, securely, and at scale. This is a hands\-on engineering position where you will contribute directly to the systems and frameworks that underpin the entire organisation’s delivery lifecycle. You will work across multiple domains, including cloud infrastructure, CI/CD, developer experience, automation, container orchestration, event streaming and observability. Your focus will be on creating robust, reusable, and scalable platform capabilities that remove friction, standardise best practices, and support a modern engineering ecosystem. By enabling teams with well‑designed tooling and infrastructure, you will help accelerate delivery, reduce operational overhead, and increase reliability across the organisation. **Who we are** Sustainability software specialist, AMCS, is headquartered in Ireland, with offices in Europe, the USA, and Australasia. With over 1,300 highly skilled employees across 22 countries, we specialize in delivering technology solutions to facilitate a carbon neutral future. **What we do** Our innovative SaaS solutions increase efficiency and boost sustainability in resource\-intensive industries. Over 5,000 customers across 23 countries already benefit from our Performance Sustainability software, ensuring we deliver practical solutions for improved profitability and environmental resilience across the globe. **Our people:** AMCS offers team members more than just a job, but an opportunity to map out a career with a company that is growing, evolving and setting out new ways of working that are having a positive impact on the world around us. AMCS was established in Ireland and holds onto those local roots and ‘start\-up’ mentality with a culture of connection. Connection to our work, our customers, our colleagues and our community that creates a working environment that fosters openness, collaboration and creativity. **Key Responsibilities:** Platform architecture development * Design, build, and maintain scalable platform services, tooling, and automation to support product engineering teams. * Develop high\-quality, reusable frameworks and infrastructure components. * Contribute to and influence platform architecture, technology selection, and long\-term roadmap. Cloud Infrastructure Reliability * Build and maintain cloud\-native infrastructure using IaC (e.g., Terraform, Bicep). * Implement highly reliable, resilient, and secure systems following SecOps and DevOps best practices. * Drive application improvements in observability (metrics, logging, tracing) and system reliability. Developer Experience Enablement * Develop internal tools, automation, and workflows that improve engineering productivity. * Partner with engineering teams to understand friction points and remove barriers. * Champion best practices around CI/CD, event streaming, testing automation, and deployment strategies. Security Compliance * Integrate security controls and guardrails into platform capabilities. * Collaborate with security teams to ensure compliance and safe\-by\-default designs. Collaboration Leadership * Mentor and guide product engineers in adopting platform systems and best practices. * Contribute technical discussions, design reviews, and cross\-team initiatives. * Drive a culture of continuous improvement, engineering excellence, and ownership. **Qualifications and personal attributes** * 5\+ years of software engineering experience, with expertise in building platforms, infrastructure, or foundational systems. * Proficiency in one or more programming languages (e.g., Go, Python, TypeScript, C\#, Java). * Deep experience with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or GCP). * Experience with event streaming * Good background in Infrastructure as Code (Terraform). * Expertise with CI/CD systems (Azure DevOps). * Experience with containers and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes). * Solid understanding of distributed systems, networking, and system design. * Experience deploying and automating Confluent Kafka infrastructure (self\-managed or cloud) using IaC and modern DevOps practices, with strong understanding of partitioning, replication, and stream processing patterns. * Experience with observability tooling (OpenTelemetry, Grafana, Datadog, etc.). * Experience building internal developer platforms (IDPs) or platform\-as\-a\-product. * Familiarity with security hardening, zero\-trust architectures, or cloud governance. * Experience working in high\-scale, high\-availability environments. \#LI\-JR1

title: CRM Developer
description: **CRM Developer** **Location** Dublin Airport, County Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid) **Department** Group IT **Reporting to** Group Director of Digital \& BI **Our Business** Glen Dimplex is a privately\-owned international group headquartered in Ireland across four main product areas: Heating \& Ventilation, Consumer Appliances, Flame and Precision Cooling. We are one of the world's leading manufacturers of electric heating appliances and a global leader in the development and delivery of low carbon solutions for space and water heating, cooling, and ventilation. Our Purpose is to be leader in the transition to a sustainable world by empowering our customers in their everyday lives. This purpose drives our teams in the work we do to help customers transition through using our products and services. The organisation is on a journey to consolidate and rationalise its disparate and standalone business application systems and data into its Dynamics Platform across commercial, marketing, customer service, field service and other business functions. **Role Purpose** As a CRM Developer you will be responsible for designing, developing, testing, and implementing custom solutions and configurations within the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Customer Relationship Management (CRM) platform. This role demands a deep understanding of Dynamics 365 modules, as well as proficiency in software development, business process optimisation, integration, and support. You will work closely with business customers/stakeholders to transform business requirements into functional technical solutions that drive organisational growth and efficiency. **Key Accountabilities** **Solution Design \& Development:** * Work with the business to define requirements and features * Analyse the business requirements and design robust, scalable, and secure CRM features and solutions using Microsoft Dynamics 365\. * Develop custom workflows, plugins, and integrations to enhance the platform’s capabilities. **Customisation** * Customise Dynamics 365 CRM entities, forms, views, dashboards, and business process flows to meet specific business needs. * Employ out\-of\-the\-box tools and custom code when necessary. **Integration:** * Design and implement seamless integrations between Dynamics 365 CRM and other enterprise systems, including ERP (SAP and Biz21\), third\-party applications, and cloud services (such as Microsoft Azure). **Data Migration:** * Plan and execute data migration strategies from legacy systems to Dynamics 365 CRM, ensuring data integrity, mapping, and transformation meet business objectives. **Testing \& Quality Assurance:** * Develop and execute unit, integration, and system tests to ensure solutions meet quality standards and functional requirements. **Support \& Maintenance:** * Provide on\-going support, troubleshooting, and maintenance for the Dynamics 365 CRM environment. * Resolve issues, apply updates, and ensure system reliability and performance. **Documentation:** * Produce and maintain comprehensive technical documentation, including solution designs, configuration notes, integration guides, and user support materials. **User Training \& Support:** * Assist in developing training materials and provide technical support to end users, ensuring smooth adoption of new features and enhancements. **Compliance \& Security:** * Implement best practices for data security, user access management, and compliance with relevant data protection regulations (such as GDPR). **Continuous Improvement:** * Stay current with the latest Dynamics 365 CRM capabilities, updates, and industry trends. Identify areas for process improvement and propose innovative solutions. **Key Skills \& Experience** **Essential** * Hands\-on experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 CRM, including modules such as Sales, Customer Service, Marketing, and Field Service. * Proficiency in Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI, Data Flows). * Strong knowledge of Microsoft Dataverse (Common Data Service). * Experience with programming languages such as C\#, .NET, JavaScript, and TypeScript. * Familiarity with web technologies (HTML, CSS, REST APIs, SOAP, OAuth). * Experience developing custom plugins, workflows, and integrations for Dynamics 365 CRM. * Solid understanding of relational databases, particularly Microsoft SQL Server. * Expertise in data migration tools and methodologies (SSIS). * Knowledge of security, authentication, and authorisation best practices within Dynamics 365\. **Desirable** * Microsoft Certified: Dynamics 365 Fundamentals (CRM) certification. * Microsoft Certified: Power Platform Functional Consultant Associate or Developer Associate. * Experience with Azure Logic Apps, Azure Functions, and Azure Service Bus. * Background in integrating Dynamics 365 CRM with other Microsoft services (e.g., SharePoint, Teams, Outlook). * Experience with DevOps tools and processes, such as Azure DevOps or Git. * Experience with project management and service desk tools (JIRA, FreshService) * Exposure the AI related technologies (e.g. Virtual Agents, Azure AI) **Qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Business Administration, or a related field (or equivalent experience). * 5 years of experience in a CRM development, or a related role. * Relevant certifications (e.g. Dynamics 365 Fundamentals (CRM), Power Platform Developer Associate (PL‑400\), Power Platform Solution Architect Expert (PL‑600\)) are a plus alongside technical certifications in cloud platforms, automation tools or enterprise systems. **Personal Attributes** * Outcome‑focused and pragmatic, with a strong bias towards delivery * Comfortable challenging existing ways of working * Structured thinker with strong title: Chemistry Expert (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Chemistry Expert (AI Training)** **About The Role** What if your years of chemistry expertise could directly shape how AI reasons through the most complex scientific problems in the world? This is a fully remote, flexible contract role built for working researchers, postdocs, faculty, and industry scientists who want meaningful work on their own schedule. No prior AI experience needed — just deep chemistry knowledge and a commitment to scientific rigor. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design and solve advanced chemistry problems spanning organic, inorganic, physical, and computational chemistry * Review and evaluate AI\-generated scientific content for accuracy, depth, and reasoning quality * Develop complex, real\-world problem statements that challenge and improve AI model reasoning * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts * Ensure scientific rigor, clarity, and precision across all deliverables * Apply computational tools or coding skills where relevant to problem design and validation **Who You Are** * Hold a Master's or PhD in Chemistry or a closely related field * Deep expertise in at least one area: organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Comfortable working with Python or computational chemistry software (e.g., Gaussian, VASP) * Exceptionally detail\-oriented with strong scientific communication skills * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently and asynchronously * Fluent in English **Nice to Have** * Experience with data annotation, scientific evaluation, or quality assurance workflows * Background in AI, machine learning, or cheminformatics * Familiarity with molecular modeling, reaction mechanisms, or materials science * Experience writing for scientific publication or peer review **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside leading research labs and AI teams * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Freelance autonomy with the structure of meaningful, task\-based scientific work * Apply your expertise to problems that directly influence how AI understands science * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Chief Operating Officer
description: **About Taxually** Taxually is a fast\-growing VAT and indirect tax compliance technology company serving 10,000\+ clients across 105\+ jurisdictions. We combine deep tax expertise with modern technology to simplify one of the most complex areas of global commerce. We're at an inflection point — scaling rapidly, embedding AI into our workflows, and building the infrastructure to support the next phase of growth. **The Role** We're looking for a Head of Operations to own the operational backbone of the company. This person will be responsible for ensuring Taxually runs efficiently, scales sustainably, and delivers consistently for clients — bringing structure and rigour to a business that's growing fast and evolving its operating model. This is a hands\-on leadership role. You'll work across Tax Operations, Customer Success, Business Support, and internal process design to drive throughput, quality, and cost efficiency. You'll partner closely with Product, Engineering, and the commercial team to ensure operational capacity keeps pace with growth and that we're deploying automation and AI where it matters most. **What You'll Do** * Operational leadership. Own the day\-to\-day operational engine — tax filing operations, client onboarding workflows, customer support delivery, and internal business support functions. Set and hold the standard for SLAs, turnaround times, and quality across every operational touchpoint. * Process design and continuous improvement. Audit and redesign core processes end\-to\-end. Identify bottlenecks, eliminate waste, and build repeatable playbooks that reduce manual effort and improve consistency as we scale across jurisdictions. * AI and automation strategy. Champion the integration of AI and automation into operational workflows. Work with Product and Engineering to prioritise, pilot, and roll out tools that reduce human\-in\-the\-loop dependency — particularly in client communications, document processing, and compliance workflows. * Performance management. Build and maintain an operational scorecard with clear KPIs across all functions. Establish a cadence of reporting and review that gives leadership real\-time visibility into operational health and flags issues early. * Team leadership. Lead, develop, and structure the operations team. Make smart decisions about headcount, role design, and capability building — balancing efficiency with the need to maintain deep domain expertise. * Cross\-functional coordination. Act as the connective tissue between commercial, product, and delivery. Ensure feedback loops exist between client\-facing teams and the teams building the product, so operational pain points translate into roadmap priorities. * Vendor and partner operations. Manage relationships with key external partners and service providers. Oversee outsourced or co\-delivered elements of the operating model to ensure quality and cost targets are met. * Scalability. Build operational models and capacity plans that support geographic expansion and new product lines without linear headcount growth. **What We're Looking For** * 8\+ years of experience in operations leadership, ideally in a B2B SaaS, fintech, or professional services environment with multi\-jurisdictional complexity. * Proven track record of scaling operations in a high\-growth company — you've built the machine, not just maintained it. * Strong process orientation. You think in systems, not tasks. You default to root\-cause analysis over firefighting. * Genuine fluency with data and metrics. You build dashboards, not just read them. * Direct experience deploying automation or AI in operational workflows. You understand what's possible and what's hype. * Excellent people leadership. You can attract, develop, and retain strong operators — and you're not afraid to make structural changes when the business requires it. * Commercial awareness. You understand that operations exists to serve the client and the P\&L, not the other way around. * Comfort with ambiguity. We're building and iterating — you'll need to make good decisions with imperfect information. **Nice to Have** * Experience in tax, accounting, or regulatory compliance operations. * Familiarity with the European VAT landscape or multi\-country compliance workflows. * Background working alongside or within product/engineering teams in a technology company. * Experience managing a transition from manual/human\-heavy operations to tech\-enabled delivery. **Why Taxually** * A category\-defining company in a massive, underserved market. * Real authority to shape how the company operates — this isn't a process\-maintenance role. * A leadership team that takes AI and automation seriously as a strategic lever, not a buzzword. * The chance to build operational infrastructure that supports global scale.

description: Civil Design Engineer **Build the Future of Power with Us! title: Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychologist
description: **Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychologist** **Salary: €63,000 \- €108,000 per annum** **Shift and schedule: Full\-time (37\.5 hours per week). Part\-time hours are also considered (minimum 3 days per week).** **Location: Blanchardstown, County Dublin** **Why Work with Evolve Ireland?** Be part of a supportive, purpose\-driven team dedicated to transforming neurodevelopmental care in Ireland and beyond. At Evolve, we offer: * A values\-led culture where clinical excellence, compassion, and staff wellbeing come first. * Continued professional development, clinical supervision, and reflective practice to support your ongoing clinical growth. * Competitive salary based on experience, with annual salary reviews and pension contributions. * Generous leave entitlement, including 28 days annual leave plus bank holidays \- and a day off for your birthday. * Private healthcare to support your physical and mental health * A company laptop and on\-call IT support to enable seamless remote and hybrid working. * Opportunities to contribute beyond clinical work via the Evolve Skills \& Research Academy: training, research \& content development. * Company events and a collaborative, multidisciplinary team passionate about improving outcomes for neurodivergent individuals and families in Ireland. **About the Role** At Evolve Ireland, we equip our clinicians with cutting\-edge tools and support so they can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional care to children, young people, and their families. You’ll join a passionate and supportive multidisciplinary team dedicated to making a meaningful difference in the lives of children and young people. Our service specialises in high\-quality, evidence\-based assessments for autism, ADHD, intellectual disability, communication and coordination difficulties under the Assessment of Need pathway, alongside private work. As a Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychologist, you’ll play a central role in delivering comprehensive neurodevelopmental assessments, contributing your expertise in diagnostic formulation, differential diagnosis, and evidence\-based practice. You will help ensure timely, accurate reports that lead to appropriate supports and improved outcomes for children, adults and their families. **As a Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychologist, you will be responsible for:** * Conducting **face\-to\-face structured observations** as part of the neurodevelopmental Assessment of Need process. * Conducting **remote developmental history interviews** with parents and carers. * Participating collaboratively in **peer MDT diagnostic discussions**, supported by senior clinicians. * Delivering clear, compassionate **feedback** to families. * Producing accurate **clinical reports,** including **diagnostic formulations**, with the help of our smart tools. * Working efficiently to meet service **Key Performance Indicators** (KPIs). * Delivering clinical supervision (where applicable). * Travelling to our clinic base(s) to conduct in\-person assessments (hybrid). **Our Ways of Working** * **Digital workflows and smart report\-writing** reduce admin time and enhance clinical impact. * **A custom\-built electronic health record system,** developed in\-house using market leading research * **Continuously enhancing internal systems**, based on employee feedback, to improve efficiency * **Ongoing training and onboarding** to ensure confidence in all tech and systems * **In\-house IT and operational support**, so you're never left troubleshooting alone **Qualifications and Experience** * A recognised university degree or diploma (NQAI Level 8 equivalent) obtained with first or second\-class honours, in which psychology was taken as a major subject and honours obtained in that subject. * A postgraduate professional psychological qualification recognised by the Psychological Society of Ireland in Clinical or Counselling Psychology, OR a postgraduate professional psychology qualification validated by the Department of Health and Children in Clinical or Counselling Psychology. * For Educational Psychologists: a postgraduate qualification in Educational Psychology recognised by the PSI. * Minimum of 3 years’ experience conducting diagnostic assessments for autism and/or ADHD, or other neurodevelopmental conditions with children and young people OR/AND adults. * Strong understanding of differential diagnosis in neurodevelopmental presentations * In\-depth knowledge of DSM\-5 diagnostic criteria (or equivalent) for autism, ADHD, intellectual disability. * Experience conducting cognitive assessments for autism and/or ADHD, with children and young people OR/AND adults * Trained and experienced in the use of neurodevelopmental diagnostic tools such as ADOS\-2, ADI\-R, etc * Effective time management and caseload organisation skills, with the ability to manage competing demands and meet clinical deadlines * Highly developed written language skills used to produce high\-quality clinical reports * Well\-developed communication skills with children, young people, and their families, ensuring compassionate, clear, and professional interactions throughout the assessment process * Ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team and participate in collaborative diagnostic discussions. * Must be eligible to work in the Republic of Ireland. **About Evolve Ireland** At Evolve Ireland, we do things differently. Founded in 2014 by Dr Laura Powling, Evolve Psychology began with a bold aim to redefine what clinical and neurodevelopmental psychology means in practice. Laura’s vision was simple yet powerful: to bring care, connection, empathy, and clinical excellence together, with no compromise. What started as a small practice delivering expert witness and neurodevelopmental assessments has grown into an international independent psychology and neurodevelopmental service providing services across the UK, Ireland and beyond. In Ireland, we are nationally and regionally approved by the HSE. We also accept privately funded referrals. Our clinicians are proudly registered with CORU or affiliated with the Psychological Society of Ireland and the Department of Health. title: Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychologist
description: **Clinical, Counselling or Educational Psychologist** **Salary: €63,000 \- €108,000 per annum** **Shift and schedule: Full\-time (37\.5 hours per week). Part\-time hours are also considered (minimum 3 days per week).** **Location: Gorey, County Wexford.** **Why Work with Evolve Ireland?** Be part of a supportive, purpose\-driven team dedicated to transforming neurodevelopmental care in Ireland and beyond. At Evolve, we offer: * A values\-led culture where clinical excellence, compassion, and staff wellbeing come first. * Continued professional development, clinical supervision, and reflective practice to support your ongoing clinical growth. * Competitive salary based on experience, with annual salary reviews and pension contributions. * Generous leave entitlement, including 28 days annual leave plus bank holidays \- and a day off for your birthday. * Private healthcare to support your physical and mental health * A company laptop and on\-call IT support to enable seamless remote and hybrid working. * Opportunities to contribute beyond clinical work via the Evolve Skills \& Research Academy: training, research \& content development. * Company events and a collaborative, multidisciplinary team passionate about improving outcomes for neurodivergent individuals and families in Ireland. **About the Role** At Evolve Ireland, we equip our clinicians with cutting\-edge tools and support so they can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional care to children, young people, and their families. or Counselling Psychology, OR a postgraduate professional psychology qualification validated by the Department of Health and Children in Clinical or Counselling Psychology. * For Educational Psychologists: a postgraduate qualification in Educational Psychology recognised by the PSI. * Minimum of 3 years’ experience conducting diagnostic assessments for autism and/or ADHD, or other neurodevelopmental conditions with children and young people OR/AND adults. * Strong understanding of differential diagnosis in neurodevelopmental presentations * In\-depth knowledge of DSM\-5 competing demands and meet clinical deadlines * Highly developed written language skills used to produce high\-quality clinical reports * Well\-developed communication skills with children, young people, and their families, ensuring compassionate, clear, and professional interactions throughout the assessment process * Ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team and participate title: Commercial Graduate Rotation Program - Benelux Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Fixed\-Term Contract** **Duration:** 12\-month **Start Date: Aug 2026** The EMEA Graduate Rotation Program is a new and exciting 12 month program which welcomes ambitious graduates who would like to begin their career journey within sales and solution engineering. It is a rotational program designed to give our graduates holistic learning and practical experience across both sales development and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills and passed all required eligibility and readiness criteria, our graduates can then go on to pursue one of these two clear career paths. Our Top Graduates can potentially promote to the career path of their choice, (depending on business demand) by month 7 of the program. Our graduate program is a fully immersive program combining comprehensive learning paths, in\-role experience, customer interaction and mentorship from senior sales and solution engineering professionals. At Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundations for a successful career in sales \& solution engineering and accelerate your career at a fast pace. We are a proud member of *Fortune 100* *Best Places to Work* list for the past 12 years and a consistent winner of *Great Places to Work.* ****Who are we looking for?**** * You are fluent in English \& Dutch * You are a 2024 or 2025 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree). We consider applicants from All Academic Fields * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * Are curious, self\-motivated and focused on continuous learning * You thrive working in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable with a fast\-paced environment **What Does Our New EMEA Graduate Program Look Like** The Program is structured as follows: **Introduction Month** During your first month you will have extensive onboarding enablement in a Salesforce\-like academy, where you will: * Get immersed in the culture of salesforce and truly understand our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies and tools. * Learn critical professional skills for your career in salesforce * Structured learning calendar with sessions \& hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni **Rotations** During this Graduate Program you will have the opportunity to experience **the Sales Graduate Path** and **the Solution Engineering Graduate Path.** The structure for both are similar, for both you will spend 60% of your time immersed in the role, you will have clear tasks, clear targets and you will align with key stakeholders and the other 40% of your time will be spent on role specific learning. You will be paired with mentors/buddies with experience in the path. If during your first rotation (months 2 throughout to month 4\) are on the Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation, then you will join the Sales Graduate Rotation (from month 5 through to 7\) and vice versa. So in the these 6 months you will have exposure to both roles. Your time spent mastering the fundamentals of both these roles during these rotations will empower you to be even more productive and impactful when you eventually promote. Throughout the rotations there will be self paced certification and skills assessment. You will be required to meet all readiness eligibility criteria before you can Panel for Promotion. **An Example of what you can expect during the specific rotations** **The Sales Graduate Rotation** * You will be taught salesforce’s best in class prospecting and qualification methodologies * You will learn how to use social, digital, video, chat and phone platforms to effectively engage with Salesforce prospects * You will collaborative with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation **The Solution Engineer Graduate Rotation** * You will have the opportunity to learn our solutions at a deeper more technical level * You work closely with the solution engineering organisation supporting them in building cutting edge cutomer demonstrations * You will learn the art of customer discovery \& how to create a customer point of view ****What’s in it for you?**** * A clear and structured career path within our Sales and Solution Engineering organisation * Opportunity to work in an international team, within a nurturing environment * Opportunity to join our diverse internal equality groups * Comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance in your relocation\-process * Additional seven days paid time off for charitable causes \- check out our 1/1/1 model ****Map Your Future**** With our Graduate Program there are two optional career paths * The Sales Path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then into our Sales organisation as Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * The Solution Engineering path where after your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Associate Solution Engineer and rise to work at every customer level ESB, SMB, GRB. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong culture of mentoring we strive to develop industry\-leading sales and solution engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to develop your international experience in an organisation that has transformed the Enterprise Software Market with Cloud Computing ****Salesforce in Dublin**** When we opened our first office in Ireland in 2000 we were one of the first digital companies to locate here. Today, our Dublin office has grown into a leading technology hub, and Salesforce is the global leader in customer relationship management (CRM), bringing companies of every size and industry together with their customers. Our new Salesforce Tower Dublin will bring our dynamic, multinational team together in the vibrant Silicon Docks area. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Commercial Litigation Associate
description: **The Opportunity** Clark Hill Dublin is seeking a Commercial Litigation Associate (5\-6 years PQE) with a strong interest in business\-critical commercial disputes before the Commercial Court and High Court. The role will involve working with lead Partners on an array of litigation to include shareholder and contractual disputes, urgent injunctions, professional negligence claims, and complex financial litigation, all within the Commercial and High Court’s accelerated case\-management framework. This is an exciting opportunity to join a significant transatlantic firm (with 31 offices and over 800 lawyers) at our Dublin city centre office. Successful candidates will be detail\-oriented and organised and will possess a solid work ethic. Candidates must have excellent interpersonal skills, show ability and willingness to work in a collaborative team culture. Additionally, strong candidates will have excellent academic credentials and demonstrated experience in a similar role. All candidates must be currently qualified to practice in Ireland. **Key Responsibilities** * Manage a busy caseload of commercial litigation and dispute resolution matters from inception through to resolution. * Act in High Court and Commercial Court proceedings, including drafting pleadings, motions, and affidavits. * Advise clients on contentious commercial issues, including contractual disputes, shareholder disputes, and business\-critical litigation. * Develop and implement dispute resolution strategies, including mediation, arbitration, and negotiated settlements. * Represent clients in case management hearings, trials, and interlocutory applications. * Work closely with senior partners and clients on complex, high\-value, and often urgent matters. * Build and maintain strong client relationships, ensuring a high standard of service delivery. **Candidate Profile** * Qualified Solicitor with minimum 5 years’ PQE in commercial litigation or dispute resolution. * Proven experience handling High Court litigation and Commercial Court matters is essential. * Strong technical expertise across commercial disputes and litigation procedures. * Demonstrated ability to manage a full caseload independently while contributing to team\-based matters. * Excellent drafting, analytical, and advocacy skills. * Commercially aware with a pragmatic and solutions\-driven approach to dispute resolution. * Strong communication skills with a client\-focused mindset. The salary range for this position at the time of posting is €90,000 \- € 100,000 annually, based upon a full\-time work schedule. Actual compensation will be influenced by various factors, including but not limited to employee qualifications, relevant experience, skill sets, training, internal equity, and market data. This position is eligible for an annual discretionary bonus. In addition, Clark Hill offers employees the following benefits in annual leave, health insurance, life insurance, contributory pension, annual Taxsaver ticket covered by the firm, social committee programme, health and wellness programme, and an employee assistance program. Clark Hill is an AmLaw 200 law firm with 30 offices across the United States, Ireland, and Mexico. With 700\+ attorneys, our value proposition is simple. We offer our clients an exceptional team, dedicated to the delivery of outstanding service. We recruit and develop talented individuals and empower them to contribute to our rich diversity of legal and industry experience. We work in agile, collaborative teams, partnering with our clients to help them reach and exceed their business goals. Clark Hill is committed to an aggressive growth strategy through the hiring of associates, counsel, and members who fit our culture, embrace the goals of our strategic plan, and bring the skills and experience to our firm that are critical to serving the needs of our clients. Successful Clark Hill employees have a track record of solid academic credentials, along with strong interpersonal and communication skills. Clark Hill is a collaborative environment which provides for both a professional and personally rewarding career.

title: Commercial Support Executive
description: **The Role:** This position involves providing comprehensive administrative and operational support to the wholesale commercial team, contributing to the achievement of sales targets, and maintaining strong internal and external relationships. * Permanent Role * Location: Citywest **Key Responsibilities / Outcomes** * Support the activities of the Commercial team when dealing with manufacturers on a day to day basis. * Administration \& product set up duties to include data maintenance \& clean up activity as required. * Manage customer \& manufacturer queries and ensure they are logged and dealt with in a professional, timely manner. * Manage customer pricing queries in conjunction with the Uniphar Finance team. * To schedule and produce reports to required timelines and perform data analysis. * Liaise with all departments across the organisation to optimise the commercial offering and the pharmacy experience of service from the business. * Be day to day contact for a number of key customer group * Be main conjugate between Operations and Retail groups in managing cross orders, promotional stock and ad hoc queries. * Actively participate and contribute to business development and project initiatives as required. * Report weekly, monthly and quarterly both formally and informally, to an agreed timetable and format. * Operate as a team player and positively contribute to achieving growth targets of the business. * Support activities, including order processing, liaising with Operations on deliveries and dispatch **Requirements** * 1\-2 years experience in administration or operational support * Strong Excel skills * SAP experience is an advantage * Strong organisation skills * Excellent Communication skills **Key Outcomes and Responsibilities** **Strategic Partnership:** * Collaborate with business leaders to understand operational priorities and develop HR strategies to meet organisational goals in line with focus projects. * Provide guidance on workforce planning, talent management, employee engagement, and organisational design. * Serve as a ‘’consultant’’ on employee relations, performance management, and change management processes. **Project Management:** * Develop and deliver on detailed project plans, timelines, and budgets to ensure successful project delivery on time and in budget where applicable. * Coordinate cross\-functional teams and stakeholders, providing updates and resolving project challenges. * Review and analyse project outcomes and recommend improvements for future initiatives. * Utilise data and insights to assess project outcomes and refine approaches. **Talent and Workforce Development:** * Partner with leaders to design and implement strategies for talent acquisition, development, and retention on key change management projects. * Conduct training sessions, information sessions and workshops on HR\-related topics and initiatives project specific. **Data\-Driven Insights:** * Use HR analytics to identify trends, risks, and opportunities, providing actionable recommendations to leadership. * Monitor key HR metrics to track the effectiveness of initiatives and projects. **Compliance and Best Practices:** * Ensure HR policies and procedures comply with employment legislation. * Maintain knowledge of best practices in HR and contribute to continuous improvement efforts. * Ensure clear, transparent, and consistent messaging across the division **Skills \& Experiences** * Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field (Master’s preferred). * Proven experience as an HR Business Partner or in a similar role, with a focus on managing HR projects and change management. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate across all levels of the organization * Excellent project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders effectively. * Strategic thinker with the ability to translate business objectives into actionable HR initiatives. * A progressive, non\-bureaucratic approach to HR * An ability to work in an agile way. Comfortable with ambiguity. * Enjoy working in an entrepreneurial culture that moves quickly and requires rapid response and the ability to make quick, sound decisions. * In\-depth knowledge of HR principles, practices, and legal requirements * Strong understanding of change management principles and experience leading organisational change initiatives * Ability to collaborate and build strong cross\-functional relationships. * An approach of listening carefully and asking questions to understand the why and the how of our current state while partnering to build our future state. * Patience and persistence in driving projects over the finish line against a landscape of regularly changing priorities. * Focused always on business impact and colleague experience. **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: Commissioning Engineer
description: **Commissioning Engineer – Dublin** (Nationwide Travel Required) Design Build Search is currently seeking a **Commissioning Engineer** based in the Dublin/Meath/Kildare area to join our clients growing team. The company delivers projects nationwide, and the successful candidate will be comfortable with regular travel across Ireland as required. This is a fantastic opportunity for a recent electrical engineer graduate looking to become part of an established, close\-knit team with a strong reputation for delivering high\-quality projects. **Responsibilities** * Commission smoke and natural ventilation systems in accordance with project specifications and EN12101 standards. * Interface with BMS systems via Modbus/BACnet gateways. * Configure and test D\+H control panels (CPS\-M), actuators (e.g., CDC series), and accessories. * Perform site acceptance tests and final handover inspections with clients and consultants. * Produce commissioning reports, operation manuals, and compliance documentation. * Collaborate with project managers and installation teams to ensure timely delivery. * Assist in fault finding and on\-site technical support as required. **Requirements** * Electrical Engineering qualification (Level 6\+ or equivalent). * 1 year of experience commissioning ventilation or building automation systems is desired * Strong understanding of electrical schematics and low\-voltage systems (24V DC). * Experience with control panels, smoke ventilation, or natural ventilation systems preferred. * Familiarity with EN12101\-2 / EN12101\-10 compliance is an advantage. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Full clean driving licence if you’re interested in learning more, apply with your CV today for a confidential discussion.

title: Compliance Analyst
description: **Role Summary** The Compliance Analyst will report to a Senior Compliance Manager on the Compliance team. They will be responsible for carrying out periodic tests in line with the Compliance monitoring plan. Testing will include key investor protection areas under MIFID II such as Suitability, Appropriateness, Product Governance and Client Categorisation among other areas. The Analyst will have the opportunity to interact with a wide range of business stakeholders in the role and provide compliance feedback and recommendations. The Analyst will present their work to the team at regular compliance monitoring meetings and contribute to enhancing the Cantor Fitzgerald investor protection framework on an ongoing basis. The Analyst will be responsible for reviewing marketing materials and will assist with other Compliance related tasks as required. Under The Central Bank of Ireland’s (CBI) Individual Accountability Framework (IAF) established under The Central Bank (Individual Accountability Framework) Act 2023 (IAF Act), as an expected Control Function holder, you have a Duty of Responsibility under the CBI Common Conduct Standards. These standards include: * acting with honesty and integrity, * acting with due skill, care and diligence, * co\-operating in good faith and without delay with the Central Bank of Ireland * acting in the best interest of customers and treating them fairly and professionally * operating in compliance with standards of market conduct and trading venue rules

title: Compliance Officer
description: **About Waystone** Waystone is a leading asset\-servicing solutions provider of institutional governance, administration, risk and compliance services to financial institutions. With over 25 years’ experience and a comprehensive range of specialist services to its name, Waystone helps our clients structure, operate and grow through our expertise, innovation and digitisation, backed by the operational scale to support global expansion. **Essential Duties And Responsibilities** * Interpret and apply applicable laws, regulations, and regulatory guidance, with particular emphasis on AML/CFT requirements, escalating issues where appropriate * Monitor regulatory developments and support the implementation of new or revised regulatory requirements * Provide day‑to‑day compliance support and guidance to internal stakeholders across Operations, Risk, Legal, and other internal functions. * Act as a point of contact for day‑to‑day compliance queries, escalating more complex issues as required. * Support business initiatives, including new products, outsourcing arrangements, and change projects. * Draft, review, and maintain compliance and AML/CFT policies and procedures, ensuring documentation remains accurate and up to date. * Support the communication and implementation of policy and procedural updates across the business. * Support the execution of the Compliance Monitoring Programme, including thematic reviews, control testing, and ongoing compliance reviews, in line with approved plans. * Support the preparation and timely submission of regulatory filings, reports, and responses to regulatory information requests or inspections. * Assist in the preparation of compliance reports and materials for senior management, Group Compliance, Risk Committees, and the Board. * Review alerts, support investigations, and escalate suspicious activity in line with established procedures * Contribute to AML risk assessments and related reporting * Assist in identifying, logging, and investigating compliance breaches, and support remediation and follow‑up actions. * Maintain accurate, complete, and audit‑ready compliance records, registers, and supporting documentation. * Assist in the development and delivery of compliance and AML/CFT training, ensuring training records are properly maintained. * Promote awareness of regulatory and compliance obligations within the business. * Attend client and other external stakeholder meetings as the Compliance representative, contributing to due diligence reviews on compliance matters. * Represent the compliance function on external and internal working groups and committees, as required. * Support junior team members and contribute positively to team knowledge‑sharing and continuous improvement initiatives. The successful candidate will be a proactive and self‑motivated professional, capable of delivering high‑quality compliance support within a dynamic and fast‑paced regulatory environment. **They Will Demonstrate** * Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage competing priorities effectively * A collaborative, solution‑oriented approach and the confidence to engage constructively with stakeholders at all levels * Excellent critical thinking, research, and interpretative skills, with the ability to apply regulatory requirements pragmatically * A high level of attention to detail and the ability to produce accurate, clear, and well‑structured work **Experience** * Minimum 5 years’ experience in a funds, fund administration, or regulated financial services compliance environment * Strong understanding of regulatory requirements applicable to the funds sector, with particular emphasis on AML/CFT * Experience in the design, implementation, and execution of: + Compliance policies and procedures + Compliance monitoring programmes **Education \& Qualifications** * Relevant compliance, AML, or financial crime qualification * University degree in Business, Law, Governance, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional qualifications or industry experience

title: Data Quality Analyst
description: **About us:** Amach is an industry\-leading technology driven company with headquarters located in Dublin and remote teams in UK and Europe. Our blended teams of local and nearshore talent are optimised to deliver high quality and collaborative solutions. Founded in 2013, Amach was created to solve a specific problem in aviation: too much complexity, too little usable intelligence. We help airlines modernise their operating model using cloud, data and Al\-delivered by teams with deep aviation domain expertise. Our goal is to maximize airlines' operational efficiency by optimizing resource use, reduce costs and increase customer experience and satisfaction. We are seeking a **Data Quality Analyst** to be embedded with our customer, where you will drive improvements in data quality, trust, and accountability across their organisation. Working closely with the customer's teams, you will identify critical data issues, define and monitor quality rules, investigate root causes, and partner with data owners and stewards to deliver sustainable improvements. This is a hands\-on role that combines data quality management, governance, and stakeholder engagement to support the customer's strategic data objectives. ***Please note** : this is a fully remote role.* **Key responsibilities \& duties include:** * Gather and document business requirements for data quality monitoring and governance initiatives working with the customer's stakeholders * Support the triage, prioritisation, investigation, and remediation of data quality issues and incidents across the customer's data landscape * Assist with the maintenance, adoption, and continuous improvement of the customer's enterprise data catalogue * Build strong working relationships with the customer's business stakeholders, data owners, and data stewards to support data quality improvement initiatives * Manage stakeholder communications throughout the lifecycle of data quality projects and initiatives with the customer's teams * Analyse the customer's existing databases, systems, and data sources to identify opportunities for improving data quality * Produce clear and comprehensive documentation, including business requirements, data quality rules, metrics, and reports * Collaborate closely with the customer's Agile delivery teams, including Product Owners, Data Engineers, and Product Managers * Contribute to the continuous improvement of the customer's data governance processes, standards, and operating procedures * Support governance activities relating to audit, risk, compliance, and regulatory requirements * Measure and report on data quality KPIs and business outcomes for the customer's leadership * Support the implementation and adoption of data governance policies and standards across the customer's organisation **Required Experience:** * Minimum 3\+ years' experience in a Data Analyst, Data Quality Analyst, Data Governance, or related role * Strong SQL and data analysis capabilities * Experience working with data quality management and data governance programmes * Experience identifying, investigating, and resolving data quality issues * Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills with the ability to influence and collaborate across multiple groups * Experience creating business and technical documentation * Ability to facilitate workshops and work effectively with both business and technical teams * Strong organisational skills with excellent attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy * Proven ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver high quality work to deadlines * Self motivated, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast paced environment * Strong analytical and problem solving skills * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Positive, collaborative, and solution focused approach * Ability to work independently while contributing effectively as part of a team * Relevant third level qualification in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Mathematics, Engineering, or a related discipline **Nice to have:** * Experience with Data Observability and Data Quality tools such as Monte Carlo, Soda, Informatica, or similar platforms * Experience working with Data Catalogue platforms such as Atlan, Collibra, Microsoft Purview, or Databricks Unity Catalog * Experience within finance, customer, operational, or enterprise data environments * Experience working in Agile delivery environments * Knowledge of AI governance frameworks and best practices * Experience using Jira and Confluence * Experience with Power BI, Snowflake, Databricks, and modern cloud data platform **What’s in it for you:** * An opportunity to join a fast\-growing company * Options for career advancement * Learning and development opportunities * Flexible working environment * Competitive salaries based on experience **Equal Opportunity Employer:** Amach is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions on the basis of merit. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. This job description is intended to convey essential responsibilities and qualifications for this role, but it is not an exhaustive list of tasks that an employee may be required to perform. If you are passionate about driving customer success, advising on strategic solutions, and contributing to product innovation, we would love to hear from you! **Not for you?** Check out all of our open positions in our **careers page** and follow us on **LinkedIn** for future opportunities. P.S. Share this with friends and co\-workers! Don't be afraid they'll steal it from you, if you're amazing and smart we'll find a role for you. We are growing fast and we are always looking for talented people. At Amach, we strive to be an inclusive community of open\-minded individuals with different backgrounds and we are committed to fostering, cultivating and preserving a culture of diversity, equity and inclusion. We strongly believe that a diversity of experience and background is essential to create a fulfilling environment and better solutions for our people and our customers. All Amach employees and contractors are expected to honour this policy and act to ensure that every individual is respected in the workplace. **Your personal data** Amach will process your personal information in accordance with the EU's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). We will comply with data protection law and principles, which means that your data will be: * Used lawfully, fairly and in a transparent way * Collected only for valid purposes and not used in any way that is incompatible with those purposes * Relevant to the purposes we have told you about and limited only to those purposes * Accurate and kept up to date * Kept only as long as necessary for the purposes we have told you about * Kept securely If you would like to contact us about your data, please use the following address: info@amach.com

You will understand customer challenges, at a strategic level and work with vendors/service partners to build end\-to\-end solutions that could encompass both on\-premise infrastructure and private cloud platforms. **Role Description:** As a member of the Technical Pre\-Sales function of Business Solutions and Services, you will be pivotal to the overall growth of Bechtle UK\&I’s Datacentre pillar. You will have a good understanding of 3 Tier and 2 Tier, storage, back up and disaster recovery technologies, with a knowledge of hybrid cloud architectures and migration considerations. **Job Role \& cost effective solution * Assess, design and present end\-to\-end Datacentre solutions wrapped with Bechtle Services * Creation of technical documentation as and when required, including HLD’s, SoW’s and proposals * Support Account Managers to deliver and hold strategic whiteboarding sessions with key customer stakeholders (virtual and F2F) * Maintain good understanding of the competitive landscape and complimenting solutions * Continuously learn and certify against key vendors within Hybrid Infrastructure such as Veeam, HPE, DELL, Microsoft Azure, VMWare, Nutanix etc. * Create and maintain relationships with customers, vendors and surrounding service partners * Conduct in\-house technology sharing to fellow peers. **Job Requirements** * Strong administration skills. * Energy, enthusiasm, creativity, determination * Natural problem\-solving ability * A strong understanding of data centre processes and procedures * Methodical thinking. * Self\-motivated and ambitious with a positive attitude. * Excellent communication and organizational skills. * Team oriented mindset. * Ability to work within a fast\-paced environment where work and fun are the key ingredients. * Ability to build strong rapport with delivery partners. * Ability to work as part of a team and display teamwork. **What we offer** * Hybrid Working (3 days in/2 days out). * Competitive salary * Location – Sandyford Business Park – Just off the M50 * Culture – Social events, Supportive, Fun, Hard working * Perks – Incentives (holidays, vouchers, lunches, spot prizes) * Top of the range technology in office and for home working (laptops, screens, Phone, etc) * Subsidised health care/medical benefits after 6 months * Annual Leave – 28 days plus B.H’s **Experience** * 3\+ years’ experience in a similar role (i.e. PreSales/consultant/engineer) * Exposure to infrastructure services and legacy transitions * Experience in recommending and designing solutions * Certifications in Azure, core infrastructure and virtualisation would be advantageous * Good understanding of our strategic vendors solutions and offerings

title: Deal Desk Analyst (Hybrid)

With more than 3 million licensed users and 13,000\+ business customers across 157 countries, we serve 67% of the Fortune 500\. ****How We Work:**** We aim to create an environment where talented individuals are empowered to excel. How we collaborate, innovate, and engage with one another is important to us. Our work is driven by 5 key principles: * One team, One mission Our collective dedication to Nitro's mission defines us. Together, we are building an environment where everyone feels like a valued part of something bigger than themselves. * Own it We take full ownership of our actions and decisions. We empower one another to lead with confidence, creativity, and a solutions\-focused mindset. * Accountable to our customers We are dedicated to our customers and take our commitments seriously. We do what we say we are going to do. * Excellence in execution Driven by passion and precision, we exemplify excellence in our delivery with innovative, top\-quality results. * Be bold, fail fast, learn faster We learn as we grow, dare to try, and bravely question. We are not chasing perfection but forever iterating towards it. These guiding values shape our approach to work, fostering a culture where everyone is inspired to contribute their best. ****The Role:**** The Deal Desk Analyst partners closely with Sales, Finance, and other cross\-functional teams to ensure all deals are structured, approved, and executed efficiently and in compliance with company policies. This role plays a critical part in enabling scalable growth by improving deal quality, streamlining processes, and supporting the end\-to\-end quote\-to\-cash lifecycle. ****What You'll Be Doing:**** * Act as a trusted advisor to Sales, guiding contract structuring and negotiation strategy, including pricing and compliant deal structuring alternatives * Manage the processing and closing of daily sales transactions using Salesforce CPQ and billing systems * Deliver customer licensing communications and support the setup of customer admin portals * Coordinate with stakeholders to seek input and approvals for complex deals * Assist sales teams with RPF/RFQ responses, including helping to maintain common questions policies * Help evolve deal desk processes, improving how we manage non\- standard deals and customer\-specific requirements What We're Looking For: * 2\+ years of operations experience with technology companies in functions such as Deal Desk , Revops or SalesOps with knowledge of Revenue principles and or contracts management * Comfortable working with systems such as Salesforce CPQ, Chargebee and billing systems * Curious, proactive self\-starter with strong problem\-solving skills * Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities * Commercially aware with an understanding of deal structuring principles **Why Nitro?** Along With Our Regular Benefits And Programs (including Health, Dental, Vision, And Retirement As Standard), We Are Also Very Proud To Offer a Few Additional Initiatives To Future Nitronauts: **Flex Time Off** Work\-life balance is important at Nitro, and we understand that there are events that we cannot plan for. We are proud to offer Flex Time Off to be used for holidays, spending days with your family, or appointments. **Hybrid Work** Our team embraces the hybrid work model, appreciating its blend of flexibility and structure. We combine three days of in\-person collaboration at our global offices in Toronto, Dublin, Antwerp, Porto, and Melbourne with the convenience of two days of remote work each week. ****Benefits:**** Nitro provides all employees with a comprehensive benefits package that includes health insurance, dental and vision coverage, and wellness perks. We also offer pension/401k matching, along with many other country\-specific benefits. *Nitro strongly encourages applications from everyone regardless of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status or disability status. We provide an accessible candidate experience and invite you to request any accommodations or adjustments throughout the interview process and beyond.*

title: Design Engineer (Civil)
description: **Design Build Search** is seeking a **Design Engineer (Civil)** on behalf of our client based in Dublin 15\. This opportunity is suited to candidates who holds a minimum Level 8 Honours Degree in Civil or Structural Engineering, with approximately 3 to 6 years’ post\-graduate experience. **Responsibilities:** Working as part of a collaborative engineering team, and with guidance from senior staff, the successful candidate will: * Engage with clients, under the direction of the Project Manager, to deliver design solutions that meet project requirements. * Review and provide technical oversight of documentation and designs prepared by Graduate Engineers. * Prepare and check preliminary designs, technical reports, and detailed design drawings/specifications using a range of industry\-standard software packages. * Coordinate and manage the procurement process for Site Investigations and Building/Civil Engineering contracts. * Support Project Managers in the preparation of tender documentation and the evaluation of submitted tenders. * Assist in the administration of construction contracts, ensuring compliance with design intent, programme timelines, and budgetary constraints. * Report to and take direction from their Line Manager, Project Managers, and senior project team members as required. **Requirements:** * Strong written and verbal communication skills in English, along with effective interpersonal abilities. * Proficiency in AutoCAD 2D, with a willingness and aptitude to further develop skills in AutoCAD 3D and BIM platforms such as Autodesk Revit. * An interest in developing competence in additional software tools, including MicroDrainage, Microsoft Access, and GIS applications such as ArcGIS and MapInfo. * A working understanding of construction contract frameworks, including the Public Works Management Framework (PWMF) forms of contract. * Familiarity with relevant project and design software such as Microsoft Project, MicroDrainage, and other tools necessary to effectively perform daily responsibilities. title: Design Engineer (Electrical) REF: 2627b
description: Fingleton White provide multidiscipline engineering services for the energy industry. We provide a full range of design, construction and commissioning services and are active across many sectors including Renewables, Gas Networks, Water, Energy \& Utilities, and Asset Management. The company is responsible for planning and development of some of the most significant projects in the energy sector in Ireland. We have 8 offices across Ireland, Northern Ireland and England with over 200 employees. **Who We Are….** We have a proven track record of delivering lasting solutions which we design based on a first principles approach. Our priority is to get the engineering fundamentals right. Fingleton White is a company that has been built on sustainability. Our very first project was a hydroelectric power station. Over our 40\-year history, we have developed our own projects which have delivered CO2 emissions savings of well over 2 million tonnes. We are focussed on reducing our carbon footprint and having a positive impact on our environment and communities through our biodiversity initiatives, our energy efficiency programmes and implementing sustainable development targets. **Our Projects….** Achieving zero carbon will require a diverse range of solutions and approaches. Our clients require individualised pathways to ensure they can achieve CO2 reductions in a cost\-effective manner that reflects their specific business needs and goals. Fingleton White is a key solution provider in this complex journey. We are delivering renewables projects, carbon reduction projects, energy efficiency upgrades and improvements for our clients. **Our People….** Our people are our only asset and are our unique selling point. Our people are key to everything we do. Business and client requirements are constantly changing, it is essential that we provide the tools to allow our team to develop their skills and expertise to match these demands. We challenge our engineers to develop and progress within the organisation. Our Development and Progression Framework details how people can develop within their roles and progress their career within the organisation. Fingleton White is an Engineers Ireland's Continuing Professional Development (CPD) Accredited Employer and we have systems in place to provide employees with targeted training and experience relevant to their grade. Chartership is supported and encouraged through CPD and mentoring and is a requirement for senior engineering roles in the organisation. We will provide you with all the training you need, exposure to projects, responsibility and real engineering experience to maximise your talents as an engineer. **Position:** ------------- **The Role….** Design Engineer \- Ref: 2627b We are looking to recruit an Electrical Design Engineer to join our Energy, Utilities and Renewables design team. The successful candidates will join an established team with senior support and will have the opportunity as their career progresses to acquire a broad range of skills and experience and to work across different areas of the business. The role will provide you with essential engineering, communications and project delivery skills which are transferable across any engineering project.
