title: Graduate Architectural Technologist
description: We are looking for a Graduate Architectural Technologist to join our Dublin Office. This role is ideal for someone with strong technical expertise and a passion for high‑quality detailing. You will work closely and coordinate with architects and other building professionals to manage and ensure a project’s success. **Responsibilities \& Duties include but not limited to:** Contributing to design development from early stages through to technical delivery Preparing planning, tender, and construction information Assisting with coordination of design information and statutory submissions Participating in project meetings and, where appropriate, site involvement Strong communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a multidisciplinary setting **Requirements:** Degree in Architecture Technologist and working towards registration with RIAI. Previous experience working on medium to large scale residential projects. Thorough understanding and application of the Irish building regulations. Strong design skills and attention to detail. Strong verbal and written communication skills. Excellent Revit Skills. Excellent problem\-solving abilities with a proactive, solution\-driven approach. **What We Offer:** Opportunity to work with a high\-performing team on a diverse range of projects. Competitive salary package based on experience and qualifications. Dynamic and supportive work environment with a focus on work\-life balance. Professional growth opportunities through training and development programmes. Collaboration with industry leaders and a chance to contribute to a sustainable future. Reddy Architecture \& Urbanism is an equal opportunity employer committed to hiring a diverse workforce and sustaining an inclusive culture. Pay: €35,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Landscape Architect
description: **Company Description** Murray \& Associates is a renowned landscape architectural practice known for its work on a wide variety of projects in urban, commercial, residential, historic, and environmental contexts. The company aims to create outstanding, innovative, and environmentally responsible landscapes that provide value to clients and enjoyment to end users. Based in Dublin, Murray \& Associates takes pride in delivering projects that offer professional satisfaction and quality results. M\&A is now accepting applications from Graduate Landscape Architects. Ideally, candidates will have: • strong enthusiasm for, and a good understanding of, the landscape around us – designed and natural • a third level qualification in Landscape Architecture with Irish Landscape Institute or IFLA accreditation • strong design \& graphic skills to produce imaginative, creative drawings, presentations and models • good written and verbal communication abilities • proficiency in relevant software, e.g. AutoCAD, BIM Software, Photoshop, Illustrator, Sketchup, etc. • previous professional office experience not essential Your application should consist of cover letter, CV and three select examples of your project work to date. Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interview. The initial contract would be for 1 year. We would expect to extend the contract on completion, by agreement. Please send your application by email for the attention of Mark Boyle, Director to: mail@murray\-associates.com – maximum size of email 10 meg; please send link for larger files. * Degree in Landscape Architecture or a related field * Previous experience is desirable but not essential

title: Graduate QA Test Engineer
description: Role overview Kick\-start your career in software quality at DOCOsoft, a Dublin\-based InsurTech company whose claims management platform processes billions in data for some of the world's largest insurers. As a Graduate QA Engineer, you will learn to break complex systems before customers ever see them, build automation from day one, and grow into a well\-rounded engineer with exposure across the full software development lifecycle. You will join a collaborative QA team that plays a critical role in every release. Working alongside developers, business analysts, and DevOps engineers, you will analyse requirements, design and execute tests, develop automation, and champion quality across the organisation. At DOCOsoft, no two days look the same and we actively encourage our people to contribute ideas, challenge assumptions, and explore different areas of the business. **About DOCOsoft** DOCOsoft is the leading provider of claims management software to the London insurance market. Our platform is trusted by over 20 of the world's top\-tier insurers, handling more than £10 billion in claims annually. Founded in Dublin in 2008, we now employ over 100 people across a range of disciplines and are in an exciting growth phase \- launching our new SaaS platform, DOCOsoft Vew, and expanding into international markets. Our culture is built on five core values: Collaborative, Committed, Trusted, Creative, and Grounded. We combine Irish hard work and community spirit with a philosophy of continuous improvement, and we invest in our people so they can do their best work. **Responsibilities** * Test and validate our platform to ensure it meets the highest quality standards before every customer release. * Design, write, and maintain automated test suites using Playwright. * Analyse requirements and user stories to create detailed, traceable test cases. * Investigate, reproduce, and clearly document defects so development teams can resolve issues efficiently. * Collaborate with developers, product owners, and DevOps engineers across multiple project teams. * Propose and pilot improvements to our testing processes, tools, and overall quality strategy. * Contribute to API and database\-level testing to ensure data integrity across our integrations. * Manage your time effectively across concurrent projects, communicating progress and risks proactively. **What You'll Learn In Your First Year** We are investing in your growth from day one. In your first twelve months, you can expect to: * Gain hands\-on experience building Playwright automation suites on real client projects. * Develop a strong understanding of the insurance domain — how complex claims data is received, processed, and presented to end users. * Learn how CI/CD pipelines work and how quality gates protect production environments. * Pair with senior QA engineers and developers through a structured mentorship programme. * Carry out Non\-Functional testing activities (performance testing and security testing. * Participate in Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives. * Present your findings and testing insights to cross\-functional teams. * Explore other areas of the business — we encourage involvement in DevOps, product, and innovation initiatives. **Key Requirements** We value curiosity, initiative, and a genuine passion for quality over a long list of credentials. If you recognise yourself in the following, we would love to hear from you: * A degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related discipline (or equivalent practical experience such as a coding bootcamp or strong personal projects). * 0–2 years of relevant experience — internships, college projects, and personal work all count. * A natural curiosity and a knack for spotting what others miss. * Comfort reading and writing code — you do not need to be an expert, but you are not afraid of a codebase. * Strong communication skills — you can clearly explain what is broken and why it matters. * A collaborative mindset and a willingness to both give and receive constructive feedback. We realise applying for jobs can feel daunting. Even if you do not tick every box above, we encourage you to apply \- the best candidate does not always check every single requirement. Our Tech Stack **You Will Work With The Following Technologies And Tools** * C\# and/or other object\-oriented programming languages. * Client\-side technologies: JavaScript and TypeScript. * Automation frameworks: Playwright, Selenium. * SQL Server and relational database querying. * Git, Azure DevOps for version control and work tracking. * Microsoft Visual Studio Code or similar IDEs. * API testing tools: Postman, REST APIs. * Load and performance testing (JMeter, K6\). * CI/CD pipelines and build automation. * Cloud infrastructure (Azure, AWS). * Unit testing frameworks. * AI\-assisted development tools (e.g. GitHub Copilot). **Who We Are** DOCOsoft is a leading software and services provider to Lloyd’s of London and the broader London insurance market. Since our foundation, we have grown to become one of the leading insurance software specialists in the London Insurance Market. We are a growing team of over 100 colleagues based in Dublin, London, Tokyo, Portugal, Spain, India and Poland. **Here’s What We Have To Offer** DOCOsoft aspires to be a market leader in the technology sector, and we are always looking for new ways to approach projects or improve existing content. We look to hire people that will help us achieve this with hard work, enthusiasm and an expression of their own ideas. We offer our people the opportunity to impact our growing business\- everyone’s contribution matters! A team environment that is focused on the creation and delivery of great products for our clients. Exciting challenges to grow and enhance their skills and a competitive pay and benefits package including; * + 25 days Annual Leave, + Private pension, + Bonus scheme, + Private health, + Life assurance. Equal Opportunity Employer DOCOsoft is committed to building an inclusive and diverse team that represents a variety of backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. We welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates, and do not discriminate on the grounds of race, religion, gender, marital or family status, age, disability, sexual orientation, membership of the travelling community or any other basis as protected by applicable law. Should you require reasonable accommodations during any stage of the recruitment process, please let us know.

title: Graduate Quantity Surveyor
description: Overview: As an **Graduate Quantity Surveyor** at Linesight, you will work with highly experienced, culturally diverse, and passionate teams nurturing a strong social dynamic and a shared sense of achievement. You will gain vast industry experience that will open doors for you, extend your skillset and expand your perspective. We will empower, support, and enable you to take real responsibility in your role so that you can take charge of your career progression with us. For us lasting trusted relationships are everything, both with our teams and our clients. The Opportunity: In this role you will:* Attend key meetings and design reviews to monitor progress, actively collaborate on solving problems and communicate impacts to partners * Participate in site walks with GC, Cost Manager and client PM, assessing progress and identifying potential cost impacts * Effectively consolidate data to prepare detailed estimates, and ad\-hoc cost reports and analyses * Maintain the project change management logs, with detail focus and accuracy * Partner with the extended Cost Management team to complete complex client tasks * Monitor the financial close\-out on all projects including settlement of all vendors We would love to hear from you if you:* Have a degree in Quantity Surveying * Are interested in becomming chartered. We can help. * Have experience in pre\- and post\-contract cost management on varied projects * Are an excellent communicator verbally and in writing * Love a dynamic environment with the opportunity to manage your own priorities and deadlines * Thrive on building strong relationships with your team and clients through a shared purpose and sense of openness and fun All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually with video required. About us: Linesight is a highly successful global project and cost management consultancy that keeps clients coming back. And for that we have our people to thank. You see we’re not like the others. We’re different. Unique. It’s our fresh thinking and focus on what matters that has led to our evolving. We are on a journey working in some of the most exciting innovative sectors with some of the world’s most prestigious companies delivering major projects that deliver a more sustainable built environment. We have an open culture and a flat structure where you can expect to be treated with genuine care, respect, and empathy. With Linesight, you can truly discover the power of team! Diversity, inclusion and accessibility Linesight is committed to transparent, equal opportunity employment practices. We are building a diverse and inclusive organisation, accessible to all, based on having a safe culture which enables all our people to be their true selves. We are a people business, and we understand that the more inclusive we are, the happier our people and better our work will be. We will ensure that individuals with disability are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the application or recruitment process and are accommodated in the workplace. If you require assistance or accommodation of any kind, please mention this in your application, we would love to hear from you!

title: Graduate Trainee Solicitor
description: Overview: ### **About A\&L Goodbody** A\&L Goodbody is one of Ireland’s leading corporate law firms, advising national and international clients across a broad range of legal services. Headquartered in Dublin’s International Financial Services Centre, the firm works with major businesses, financial institutions, public bodies and multinational organisations across Ireland and internationally. At ALG, trainees are given the opportunity to build a strong foundation in commercial law while learning from experienced lawyers, partners and specialist teams. The firm places a strong emphasis on technical legal training, commercial awareness, collaboration, responsible business and long\-term career development.### **The Opportunity** This graduate role is designed for ambitious individuals who are interested in building a career as a solicitor in a leading Irish corporate law firm. As a Graduate Trainee Solicitor, you will gain practical legal experience across a number of practice areas, working alongside partners, associates and wider client teams. You will develop your legal, commercial and professional skills through structured seat rotations, hands\-on client work, formal training and mentoring. The programme is suited to graduates who are intellectually curious, commercially aware, collaborative and motivated to make an impact from the beginning of their career. Responsibilities: * As a Graduate Trainee Solicitor, you will: + Support lawyers and partners on client matters across corporate and commercial legal practice areas. + Conduct legal research and prepare clear, accurate summaries for internal and client use. + Draft, review and proofread legal documents, correspondence, transaction materials and briefing notes. + Assist with due diligence, document management, case preparation and transaction support. + Attend internal meetings, training sessions and, where appropriate, client meetings. + Build an understanding of Irish corporate law, business regulation and the commercial issues affecting clients. + Work collaboratively with colleagues across different departments and practice groups. + Develop strong professional habits around accuracy, confidentiality, time management and client service. + Take ownership of your learning and actively seek feedback throughout each seat. + Contribute to the wider firm culture, including knowledge sharing, pro bono, responsible business and graduate community initiatives.### **Training and Development** The programme provides structured training and development from the start of your career. Trainees can expect a combination of practical legal work, formal learning, commercial awareness training, advocacy, business skills development and practice group insights. ALG states that trainees take part in training sessions designed specifically for trainee solicitors, including legal development, commercial awareness, advocacy and business skills. You will complete rotations across different departments, helping you gain exposure to a variety of legal disciplines and identify your areas of interest. ALG’s trainee programme includes four seats across areas such as Disputes \& Investigations, Commercial Property, Corporate and M\&A, ESG, Finance, Taxation, Pensions and Pro Bono. Qualifications: * ### **What We Are Looking For** We are looking for graduates who can demonstrate: + A strong academic record and genuine interest in commercial law. + Excellent written and verbal communication skills. + Strong attention to detail and the ability to work accurately under pressure. + Commercial awareness and an interest in how businesses operate. + Curiosity, initiative and a willingness to learn. + Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. + The ability to work effectively as part of a team. + Professionalism, integrity and sound judgement. + Resilience, adaptability and a positive attitude. + Commitment to building a long\-term legal career.### **Eligibility** This role is suitable for final\-year students, postgraduates and graduates who have obtained a third\-level degree qualification. ALG’s Trainee Solicitor Programme FAQ states that final\-year students, postgraduates and those who have successfully obtained a third\-level degree qualification can apply. Applications are welcomed from both law and non\-law backgrounds, provided candidates can demonstrate a clear interest in a career in corporate law. Additional Information: ### **Why Join A\&L Goodbody?** Joining ALG as a Graduate Trainee Solicitor offers the opportunity to:* Train with one of Ireland’s leading corporate law firms. * Gain exposure to high\-quality domestic and international client work. * Learn directly from experienced lawyers and partners. * Build experience across multiple legal practice areas. * Develop both legal and business advisory skills. * Be part of a collaborative, ambitious and supportive trainee community. * Contribute to meaningful work across pro bono, responsible business and community initiatives. ### **Application Process** Candidates will typically be asked to submit an online application during the firm’s graduate recruitment period. ALG’s FAQ notes that its Milkround recruitment process generally takes place from September to October, and many trainee solicitors are also recruited through the Summer Internship Programme.

title: Housekeeper - Ireland / 7381 - Keavan`s Port Hotel, Camden Street
description: The ideal applicant We're looking for fun, enthusiastic, passionate people to join our hard\-working teams. No experience is needed, just a good attitude, a willingness to learn and a calm head under pressure. As a UK Top Employer with an award\-winning training programme, we know that we can teach you the skills you'll need to succeed. Whether you're looking for a long\-term career or just some extra shifts, if you're reliable, friendly and love working as part of a team \- this role could be perfect for you. This role is not suitable for under\-18s because of lone working. There may be other roles available, including front\-of\-house and kitchen roles. What's in it for you? * competitive rate of pay * a free meal and a drink, when working * 20% discount on all food, drinks and hotel accommodation (for you and up to three guests), when not working * €1 extra per hour, for hours worked during midnight\-5\.59am * bonus scheme \- earn up to 19% of your pay * availability of guaranteed\-hours contracts and variable\-hours contracts * paid holiday * free shares (after a qualifying period) * loyalty reward scheme The role At a glance, your role is to clean all areas of the hotel thoroughly, with the aim of enhancing the guests' experience by having excellent standards of cleanliness at all times. **Duties include:** * interacting politely with guests \- greeting them, saying 'thank you' and 'goodbye' * servicing bedrooms and all other areas of the hotel in line with our standard operating procedures * completing daily, weekly and monthly cleaning tasks, as required * following correct procedures when using cleaning chemicals and equipment * monitoring cleaning supplies and notifying the manager when more are needed * storing all laundry correctly and completing relevant documentation * adhering to stock\-control procedures and maintaining security of stock at all times * reporting any security issues, safety hazards, accidents, incidents and/or maintenance issues to the manager on duty * completing required induction, role\-specific and ongoing refresher training and contributing to continuous improvement Progression and development Most Wetherspoon team leaders and managers started as an associate. If you're looking to progress, we can support your development with: * structured on\-the\-job training. * apprenticeships and management academies. * cross\-training into other areas (bar, floor, kitchen). * opportunities to progress into team\-leader and management roles and beyond. Wetherspoon promotes an inclusive working environment where everyone is welcome. When you're at work, we want you to feel comfortable being yourself. Whatever your background, preferences or beliefs, we'll ensure that you have the skills and knowledge you need for a great career with us. If a high number of applications is received, this vacancy may close early. **HOURS PER WEEK:** 28

title: Human Resources Generalist- 4.5 days per week
description: **Location:** Weatherwell Industrial Estate, Clondalkin **Reports To:** Head of HR **Full\-Time Permanent** (4\.5 days/week). Onsite as our workforce is onsite and we support them **Hours:** 37 hours week Mon to Thurs: 8:00am \- 4:30pm. Early finish most Fridays @ 1:00pm, no weekends required. **Salary:** €50,000 to 55,000 for the 37 hours Due to continued expansion, we are hiring a Human Resources Generalist reporting to the Head of HR. The successful candidate will play a key role in delivering HR services across our Clondalkin and Cherry Orchard sites in Dublin, enabling the design and production of low voltage electrical power distribution systems for both local and multinational clients. This position is ideal for someone with minimum 3 years of experience in a dedicated HR role who wants broad, hands\-on exposure across HR and thrives on tackling a variety of challenging situations. We looking for a true generalist who is a competent, confident HR professional who can operate credibly with all employees, taking ownership of complex matters, and applying sound judgement grounded in legislation, policy, and best practice. **Key Responsibilities** **Employee Relations \& HR Advisory** * Attend weekly or bi weekly meetings with various managers regarding business progress in the context of HR ensuring compliance with Irish employment legislation and best practice. * Act as a trusted advisor, coaching leaders on HR\-related issues with a professional and introspective approach towards performance , absence management, conduct and other related matters. * Independently handling investigations such as grievance and disciplinary processes, applying the principles of fair and natural justice, ensuring compliant documentation aligned to Irish employment law and best practice. * Provide guidance to management and employees on all HR matters, * Maintain consistency in policy application and precedent, ensuring fairness and legal compliance. **Learning \& Development Delivery** * Deliver new starter induction presentations and reinductions as required. * Work closely with the Head of HR to design, deliver and evaluate in\-person and online training programs using our new LMS, covering technical, compliance, and soft\-skills training. Maintain accurate, centralized training records to support group reporting, development tracking and compliance requirements. * Support leadership development and workforce upskilling by partnering with the Leadership team and HR team to identify current and future capability needs. * Work closely with managers to identify learning needs and development opportunities across the organisation. **HR Operations \& Compliance** * Apply policy, precedent, and legislation consistently, understanding the risk and impact of decisions on the business. * Act as the first point of escalation for operational HR issues, exercising sound judgement and knowing when to step back. * Co\-own with our HR Admin the day\-to\-day HR operations across the employee lifecycle, including onboarding, contract management, probation reviews, role changes, and leavers. * Prepare and analyse HR metrics (absence, turnover, ER trends, training compliance), providing insight rather than just data. * Continuously review, draft, implement, and interpret HR policies and procedures, ensuring alignment with Irish employment legislation, codes of practice, and case law, and applying them consistently in live, day\-to\-day situations. * Work closely with the HR Admin to maintain accurate and legally compliant employee records, ensuring GDPR standards are met at all times. Ensuring documentation and processes stand up to required scrutiny. * Work closely with the HR Admin to Manage HRIS systems end\-to\-end, including data integrity, reporting, audits, and process improvements. * Ensure employment contracts, letters, and documentation are fit for purpose, legally compliant, and commercially appropriate. **Culture, Engagement \& CSR** * Coordinate and support initiatives that enhance employee engagement, wellbeing, and inclusion. * Drive contributions to corporate social responsibility programs and projects that strengthen company culture. * Champion team\-building, employee recognition programs, CSR activities and effective internal communication to foster a positive workplace environment. **Projects \& Continuous Improvement** * Own and deliver HR projects aligned with the 24\-month HR Project Plan, focused on improving employee experience, HR processes, and systems. * Present findings, analyze trends, identify opportunities, and implement practical solutions that add business value. * Collaborate with HR colleagues to ensure cohesive and coordinated project delivery across the team. **Recruitment \& Talent Acquisition** * Focus remains on broader HR responsibilities; day\-to\-day recruitment is handled by our dedicated recruiter. This role occasionally supports recruitment for specialist roles, internal promotions, or internal moves. * Advising managers on specs, selection and assessment for these specific cases, ensuring fair and effective decisions are applied. **Essential Qualifications \& Experience:** * 3 years proven HR experience in a local business operating within a multinational environment. * Broad, hands\-on experience across HR functions including employee relations, learning \& development, recruitment (supporting specialist/internal roles), and compliance. * Strong knowledge of Irish employment law, codes of practice, and HR best practices. * Experience drafting HR policies and procedures, applying them consistently to various situations. * Confident handling complex employee relations, including grievances, disciplinaries, and performance issues. * Demonstrated ability to coach and influence management in professional HR practices. * Experience delivering learning modules and development programs. * Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment. **Essential Experience \& Qualifications** * Professional HR qualification (e.g., CIPD) or 3\+ years’ equivalent experience in a broad HR role. * Experience handling employee relations, L\&D, HR operations, and compliance within a local business, it would be an advantage if this was part of an MNC environment. * Strong familiarity with HRIS systems and reporting platforms; experience with docking systems is an advantage. * Comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast\-paced, hands\-on environment. **Additional Experience \& Competencies** * Exposure to manufacturing or on\-site operational environments is an advantage. * Experience in employee engagement or culture initiatives. * Business\-minded, with a strong sense of accountability. * Policy and precedent driven, applying guidance consistently and fairly. * Highly professional, diplomatic, and introspective in communication and coaching. * Collaborative team player, able to operate independently and take ownership. * Passionate about HR as a career, motivated by variety, challenge, and responsibility. * Comfortable working on\-site and supporting operational teams directly **What We Offer** * An engaging and collaborative HR team environment. * Exposure to the full HR spectrum in a varied, fast\-paced business. * Good work life balance with a 4\.5 day week. * Opportunities to lead projects, shape culture, and develop professionally. * Regular team interactions and 1:1 coaching with your HR leader. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €50,000\.00\-€55,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * DUBLIN 22, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Will you now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status? * Are you satisfied this is a fully onsite role? Experience: * Employee relations as HR: 3 years (preferred) * leading grievance and disciplinary processes: 2 years (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Associate CIPD Recognition (preferred) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: IAM Engineer
description: Senior IAM Engineer 📍 Dublin, Ireland \| 5 Days Onsite \- South Dublin (Parking onsite) Join a global banking technology team at the forefront of Identity \& Access Management, helping to secure critical systems and digital services used across a large\-scale international environment. This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced IAM Engineer to design and deliver enterprise authentication, federation and cloud identity solutions while working with cutting\-edge security technologies and cloud platforms. What You'll Be Doing * Designing and implementing IAM solutions across cloud and on\-prem environments. * Delivering SSO, authentication and federation solutions using PingFederate, PingAccess and PingIDM. * Integrating enterprise and cloud applications using OAuth, OpenID Connect, SAML and SCIM. * Supporting Cloud IAM initiatives across Azure, AWS and GCP. * Working closely with engineering, infrastructure and security teams on large\-scale transformation programmes. What We're Looking For * 7\+ years' experience in IAM, Information Security or Security Engineering. * Strong experience with Ping Identity technologies. * Expertise in OAuth, OIDC, SAML, SCIM and LDAP. * Experience delivering Cloud IAM solutions across Azure, AWS or GCP. * Strong scripting or development skills (Java, Python, PowerShell or JavaScript). * Experience working within large, complex enterprise environments. This is a fantastic opportunity to work on high\-impact security initiatives, modern cloud identity platforms and enterprise\-scale technology programmes within a world\-class financial services organisation.

title: IT Infrastructure Engineer
description: We are hiring an IT Infrastructure Engineer to join our client’s IT team and support critical 24/7 healthcare operations in a complex enterprise environment. This is a hands\-on role covering networking, servers, virtualisation, storage, backup, security, and Microsoft infrastructure, with a strong focus on operational support, troubleshooting, and infrastructure project delivery. You’ll work closely with internal IT teams, clinical departments, vendors, and third\-party providers to ensure secure, stable, and resilient IT services that directly support patient care. This is a full time permanent position based in Dublin Sandyford, 4 days a week on site /1 day WFH Responsibilities: * Support and maintain enterprise infrastructure systems and services * Troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues across multiple domains * Support networking, server, virtualisation, backup, and security platforms * Contribute to infrastructure upgrades, migrations, and improvement projects * Participate in DR, monitoring, and operational resilience activities * Support 24/7 critical healthcare IT environments Requirements: * 4\+ years’ experience in IT infrastructure or systems engineering * Strong troubleshooting skills across enterprise environments * Broad exposure to networking, Windows/Linux, virtualisation, or security * Experience working with infrastructure incidents and projects * Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills *We can only proceed with applications from candidates fully eligible to work in Ireland*

title: IT Onsite Support Administrator
description: **WHAT YOU DO AT AMD CHANGES EVERYTHING** At AMD, our mission is to build great products that accelerate next\-generation computing experiences—from AI and data centers, to PCs, gaming and embedded systems. Grounded in a culture of innovation and collaboration, we believe real progress comes from bold ideas, human ingenuity and a shared passion to create something extraordinary. When you join AMD, you’ll discover the real differentiator is our culture. We push the limits of innovation to solve the world’s most important challenges—striving for execution excellence, while being direct, humble, collaborative, and inclusive of diverse perspectives. Join us as we shape the future of AI and beyond. **Together, we advance your career.** **The Role** We are looking to hire a permanent member of our EMEA OSS (OnSite Support Services) team. The EMEA OSS team supports employees and contractors in our 40\+ locations across the region. Our focus is on supporting client technology (primarily Windows) and Audio Video services, and on successful Day 1 onboarding for new employees. We strive for a high level of customer satisfaction through providing quality and efficicent support services, alongside the ability to adapt to the ever changing technology landscape This role will be based on site in our Dublin office and is full time in office **What You’ll Be Doing** * Providing first \& second level support to all EMEA based employees and contractors, in person or remote * Imaging and deploying laptops to support new hire and laptops refresh processes * Ensuring successful Day 1 IT onboarding for all new employees * Managing assigned Incidents and Tasks though closure, ensuring regular updates to users and efficient resolution * Support of internal meetings and events * Participation in and support of projects and technology deployments * Documentation of support processes and knowledge articles **Experience Requirements** * Minimum of 2 years hands\-on technical experience supporting Windows client technology preferably in a corporate environment * Working knowledge of laptop imaging tools such as SCCM and AutoPilot * Working experience of supporting meetings and of meeting tools \& technology (e.g. Cisco Video Conferencing, Teams \& Zoom) * Working Knowledge of mobile technology and devices (e.g. iPhone, Android \& InTune) * Working Knowledge of Linux desired but not essential * Familiarity with networking concepts (DNS, LAN, WAN). * Familiarity with Ai tools such as CoPilot and Claude desired **About You** * Bachelor’s Degree or Diploma in Information Technology or Computer Science * A strong interest in technology and experience working in a customer\-facing environment * Strong communication skills, both verbally and written * Good time management and the ability to prioritize work * A willingness to learn and adapt as technology changes *Benefits offered are described:* AMD benefits at a glance. *AMD does not accept unsolicited resumes from headhunters, recruitment agencies, or fee\-based recruitment services. AMD and its subsidiaries are equal opportunity, inclusive employers and will consider all applicants without regard to age, ancestry, color, marital status, medical condition, mental or physical disability, national origin, race, religion, political and/or third\-party affiliation, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates and will accommodate applicants’ needs under the respective laws throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.* *AMD may use Artificial Intelligence to help screen, assess or select applicants for this position. AMD’s “Responsible AI Policy” is available here.* *This posting is for an existing vacancy.*

title: IT Server & Network Hardware Support Technician
description: Onsite IT Server \& Network Hardware Support Technician Leixlip, Co. Kildare (Onsite – Data Centre Environment) 12\-month contract \| 24x7 shift pattern (4 on / 4 off – days \& nights) Start date: ASAP/TBC Pay: €18\.50 per hour We are currently recruiting experienced **Onsite IT Server \& Network Hardware Support Technicians** to join a dedicated team supporting a high\-performance data centre environment in Ireland. This is a hands\-on, physically active role working within a fast\-paced enterprise infrastructure setting, supporting critical server, storage, and network hardware operations. Why apply? * Long\-term 12\-month contract stability * Structured 24x7 shift pattern (4 on / 4 off) * Opportunity to work in a high\-spec enterprise data centre environment * Exposure to enterprise networking, servers, storage, and global support teams * Strong technical development pathway in infrastructure engineering

title: IT Solutions Architect
description: **Job Title** IT Solutions Architect **Salary** €76, 202 pa (Other arrangements may apply of you are currently a serving civil or public servant) **Tenure** 5\-year fixed term contract **Work Location** Dublin office **Closing Date** Midnight, Thursday 2nd July 2026 **Role Profile** We have an exciting opportunity in SEAI for a Solutions Architect in the IT Department which is part of the Corporate Services Directorate. The IT Department provides support across all aspects of SEAI operations, with teams focused on several areas: IT Project and Business System Programmes (PMO), Enterprise Architecture, Data Governance, IT Operations including IT Helpdesk, IT Governance, IT Change Management, and Cyber and Information Security. The successful candidate will join the current Enterprise Architect and support them in defining, maintaining, and governing the SEAIs IT systems architecture to ensure that business strategy objectives, and cost‑effective technology are aligned. Reporting to the Chief Information Officer (CIO), the successful candidate will work across the IT Department and third\-party vendors to develop and integrate ICT systems, reviewing existing systems to identify areas for improvement and monitoring the outcome of new and upgrades business systems. The successful candidate will join an enthusiastic and innovative team who are committed to the high\-quality delivery of SEAI’s ambitious ICT Strategy and customer centric digital services. **Essential Requirements** The successful candidate must be able to demonstrate: * A degree (minimum Level 8 on the NFQ or higher) in IT, Computer Science, or other relevant discipline. * A minimum of 5 years directly relevant experience working in IT Architecture design and implementation within large complex organisations (a master’s degree may account for 1\-years’ experience). * Demonstrated proficiency in system architecture principles and frameworks, software development practices, cloud platforms (Azure) and service orientated architecture (SOA), data architecture and software development practices. * Experience in establishing and operating architecture frameworks and governance. * Significant experience in stakeholder management involving a diverse range of internal and external stakeholders with complex interdependencies. * Proficiency in development methodologies such as DevOps. **Desirable Requirements** * Certificate in architecture frameworks such as TOGAF. * Experience in enterprise\-level systems design. * Experience in microservices and API first architectures. * Proficiency in the Irish Language. * Experience or knowledge of delivering positive customer experiences. **Eligibility to Work** The SEAI has a legal obligation to ensure that all employees are lawfully entitled to work in Ireland. To qualify, candidates must hold a valid visa on the date of application and where applicable to support a candidate’s application, candidates must submit a valid work permit/visa confirming permission to work in Ireland when requested. Failure to submit the required evidence, and failure to hold a valid permit/visa during the period of the contract, will result in the application and/or contract of employment being rendered void. Note in respect of UK citizens: Information regarding the Common Travel Area is available **here** .

title: IT Support Engineer - Level 2
description: **PFH Technology are currently looking for an experienced L2 IT Support Engineer to join our team in Dublin.** **Contract:** Permanent contract **Onsite**: 4 days per week onsite, work from home on Fridays **Location:** Dublin 1, with free onsite parking Prerequisites: ITIL V4, Microsoft Certifications including MD 102 **Skills/Requirements** * **Technical Skills** * **Operating Systems:** Advanced knowledge of various operating systems like Windows, macOS, and Linux. + Installing, configuring, and troubleshooting OS\-related issues. + Knowledge of Active Directory (AD), Group Policies, and domain\-related issues (for Windows environments). * **Network Troubleshooting:** + In\-depth understanding of networking concepts such as DNS, DHCP, TCP/IP, subnetting, VLANs, and firewalls. + Diagnosing and resolving issues related to routers, switches, firewalls, and VPNs. + Monitoring and maintaining network performance. * **Hardware and Software Troubleshooting:** + Ability to diagnose hardware failures (laptops, desktops, servers). + Knowledge of software deployment, updates, and patch management. * **Virtualization:** Familiarity with virtualization platforms like VMware, Hyper\-V, or VirtualBox. * **Cloud Technologies:** Understanding cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and managing cloud services like virtual machines, storage, and cloud backups. * **Scripting and Automation:** Basic scripting knowledge (PowerShell, Bash, Python) for automating routine tasks and troubleshooting complex issues. * **Advanced Troubleshooting and Problem Solving** * **Root Cause Analysis:** Ability to perform deep dives into issues to identify root causes rather than simply resolving symptoms. * **Log Analysis:** Reading and interpreting system and network logs to diagnose and resolve technical issues. * **System Performance Tuning:** Diagnosing performance bottlenecks in hardware, applications, and networks, and suggesting improvements. * **Incident Management:** Following standard ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) practices for incident escalation, documentation, and resolution. * **Communication Skills** * **Customer Service:** Communicating complex technical information in a user\-friendly way to non\-technical staff or clients. * **Collaboration:** Working effectively with Level 1 (frontline) support teams, and escalating issues to Level 3 or specialized support teams when necessary. * **Documentation:** Creating and updating technical documentation, including knowledge base articles and standard operating procedures (SOPs). * **Ticket Management:** Using ticketing systems (e.g., Jira, ServiceNow, Zendesk) to manage and resolve technical support requests. * **Security Awareness** * **Cybersecurity Knowledge:** Awareness of security best practices, threat mitigation, and basic concepts like encryption, VPNs, multi\-factor authentication (MFA), and firewalls. * **Endpoint Security:** Ensuring that workstations, laptops, and mobile devices are secured against potential threats. * **Backup and Disaster Recovery:** Familiarity with backup solutions, disaster recovery planning, and ensuring data integrity. * **Database Management** * **Basic SQL Knowledge:** Understanding of databases and SQL queries for troubleshooting database\-related issues or working with database administrators (DBAs). * **Backup and Restore:** Ability to perform basic database backups and restore operations in case of failure. * **Project Support** * **Support for IT Projects:** Participating in IT projects such as software rollouts, infrastructure upgrades, or migrations. * **Change Management:** Understanding change control processes and ensuring that system changes are well\-documented and implemented according to organizational policies. * **Remote Support Tools** * **Remote Troubleshooting:** Proficiency with tools like TeamViewer, AnyDesk, or Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) for assisting users remotely. * **Monitoring Tools:** Familiarity with network and system monitoring tools (e.g., Nagios, SolarWinds, PRTG) to proactively identify issues. * **Time Management and Prioritization** * **Task Prioritization:** Ability to prioritize tasks based on the severity and impact of technical issues. * **Multitasking:** Managing multiple incidents or service requests at the same time while ensuring timely resolution. * **Vendor and Third\-Party Support** * **Vendor Management:** Liaising with third\-party vendors for escalated technical issues related to specific hardware or software products. * **Warranty and RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) Management:** Handling warranty claims and equipment replacements through vendor support. * **Certifications:** **Essential** * **Microsoft Certifications:** (e.g., Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, Microsoft 365 Certified). * **ITIL Foundation V4:** For understanding IT service management (ITSM) processes. **Optional but Beneficial** * **CompTIA Network\+, CompTIA Security\+** for networking and security fundamentals. * **Cisco Certifications:** (e.g., CCNA for networking fundamentals). **Education** * Third Level Education Qualification is preferable. PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway. IND1

title: Junior Associate - Investment Servicing
description: Summary: The Junior Associate will assist the Middle Office team in the development of large\-scale projects for high profile platforms, from Management Companies to fund clients and work in a supportive role within the Trade Execution and Investment Servicing teams. * Adhere to and follow the middle office checklist daily, including but not limited to Corporate Actions, Data Checks, Subscriptions and Redemption activity, Unfilled Order and Pending Instructions, Failed Trades, New Order Instructions and Reconciliations. * Assist the Manager with the design and implementation of are met for an assigned portfolio of clients. * Communicate with clients to ensure satisfaction with the service provided and to resolve any potential issues. * When instructed, independently liaise with fund service providers and fund legal counsels. title: Junior Company Secretary
description: **Exciting Opportunity: Junior Company Secretary** Are you working in company secretarial, corporate administration or financial services and looking to build a career within the funds industry? We're partnering with a highly regarded governance and corporate services firm that is continuing to grow its Dublin team. This is an excellent opportunity to join a business known for its award\-winning culture, flexible working environment and commitment to employee development. The role offers the chance to work closely with investment funds and their boards, gaining exposure to a wide range of governance, company secretarial and corporate services activities while developing your technical expertise in a supportive environment. **What You'll Be Doing** * Supporting a portfolio of investment fund and corporate clients * Assisting with the organisation of board, committee and shareholder meetings * Preparing board packs, agendas and governance documentation * Drafting minutes and written resolutions * Maintaining statutory registers and corporate records * Supporting regulatory and statutory filings * Liaising directly with clients, directors, fund administrators, legal advisers and other stakeholders * Assisting with transactions, incorporations and ongoing governance projects **Why Consider This Opportunity?** * Exposure to a broad range of fund structures and investment vehicles * Direct interaction with clients, boards and senior stakeholders * Significant flexibility and autonomy in how you work * Support towards CGI and other professional qualifications * Strong mentoring from experienced governance professionals * Clear progression opportunities within a growing business * Award\-winning workplace culture focused on development and collaboration **About You** * 1\-3 years' experience within company secretarial, corporate administration, funds, financial services or a related environment * Strong attention to detail and organisational skills * Excellent communication and relationship\-building abilities * An interest in corporate governance and the funds industry * A proactive mindset and willingness to learn * This is an ideal opportunity for someone looking to establish or further develop a career in funds governance, offering exposure, responsibility and support from a team genuinely invested in your long\-term success. **What's on Offer?** * Genuine client exposure from an early stage * A highly collaborative and supportive team environment * Strong mentoring and career development opportunities * Exposure to a varied portfolio of clients and governance matters * Flexible working arrangements * The opportunity to join an award\-winning workplace culture where professional growth is actively encouraged Interested or know someone who might be ? You can email me on alex.obrien@recruiters.ie or call me on 0035315225353 **By applying to this job, your CV will be assessed by a consultant for the position described, along with considering you for other relevant­ jobs as they arise in accordance with your interest, experience and skillset. Your CV may be added to our secure applicant tracking system however your details will never be shared to any other third party without your consent. You can browse a full spectrum of job opportunities and exercise your data subject rights anytime.**

title: Junior Marketing Executive
description: **Job Title:** Junior Marketing Executive **Reports To:** Senior Marketing \& Events Specialist /Head of Global Sales **Position Overview:** We are excited to announce an opening for a Junior Marketing Executive within our team! This is a crucial role in ensuring seamless operations within Softworks. This individual must be highly motivated and detail\-oriented, as the successful candidate will be responsible for content creation, events, and marketing operations. This is an excellent opportunity for a proactive and creative individual to contribute to the success of a growing organisation and develop their marketing skills while gaining hands\-on experience. **Role Responsibilities:** **Video Production \& Content Creation:** · Create and edit video content for social media, marketing campaigns, events, and internal projects. · Record and produce video content from initial concept through to final editing and delivery. · Ensure all content aligns with brand guidelines and marketing objectives. **Market Research \& Insights:** · Monitor market trends, competitor activity, and industry developments to identify opportunities and inform marketing strategies. **CRM \& Data Management:** · Support data enrichment and database maintenance activities. · Perform data cleansing and data upload tasks to ensure CRM accuracy and integrity. **Sales \& Event Support:** · Assist with sales enablement activities for events, including preparing materials, presentations, and resources. · Support event delivery, evaluations, and post\-event reporting. **Administrative \& Marketing Support:** · Provide general marketing and administrative support across the department as required. · Assist with ad\-hoc projects and initiatives to support broader business objectives. **Continuous Improvement:** · Foster collaboration within the Softworks Group and liaise effectively with other departments and stakeholders. · Maintain compliance with international data privacy laws (e.g., GDPR). · Stay updated with the latest industry trends and best practices, particularly digital marketing trends, and AI innovations relevant to global markets. **Requirements:** **Education \& Qualifications:** · Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing or related field **Professional Skills:** * Demonstrated interest in marketing, digital content creation, and event management * Confidence in creating and producing video content, from filming through to editing and final delivery * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the confidence to engage professionally with customers and stakeholders where required * Willingness and flexibility to travel for events and business activities as required * Excellent organisational skills with a high level of attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities * Experience using video editing software and content creation tools * Proficiency in Microsoft office and Google Workspace (G\-suite) applications **Expectations:** · Demonstrate agility in a fast\-changing technological environment, being open and adaptable to unsuspecting change. · Encouraging excellence and goal achievement within Softworks, while striving for the same in your personal development. · Communicate regularly and efficiently with all stakeholders, both internal and external. · Maintain strong co\-operative relationships within the team and other departments to ensure that the organisation continues to function successfully. · Ability to analyse complex data, identify issues, and recommend solutions. · High level of accuracy and a commitment to producing high quality work. · Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present information clearly and concisely. · Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritise effectively, and meet deadlines. · Understanding of business operations and how marketing supports strategic objectives. · Strong ethical standards and a professional approach to work. **About the Softworks Group:** For over 30 years, we have been assisting companies around the world in organising the working day to make them more productive, profitable, and add value to their operations. Our globally recognised and acclaimed Workforce Management Solutions include: · Time \& Attendance/Flexible Working · Labour Scheduling \& Optimisation · Absence Management · Project Tracking · Access Control We take a customer\-centric approach to always exceed expectations and delight our clients with a standout and powerful product. Within the Softworks Group, we are more than just people that work together; we belong to a team, all working towards one common goal – to create, market, and provide unparalleled support for the world’s most advanced and effective Workforce Management Solutions. **Location:** The role will be based in Softworks HQ in Bray, Co. Wicklow. We currently have hybrid working in place, combining office\-based working with working from home. **Benefits:** · Annual Leave starting at 23\.5 days · Flexible Schedule · Hybrid Working · Workplace Initiatives to Promote Employee Wellbeing · Vibrant Social Club · Ministry of Fun · Regular Office Lunches · Cycle to Work Scheme · Pension Contributions **Salary Details:** €28,000 \- €32,000 DOE *Agency assistance is not currently required.* Pay: €28,000\.00\-€32,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Flexitime * On\-site parking * Work from home Ability to commute/relocate: * Bray, County Wicklow: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Education: * Bachelor's (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: Hybrid remote in Bray, County Wicklow

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor
description: At JJ Rhatigan, we have been delivering excellence in construction for over 70 years. As one of Ireland's leading building contractors, we are committed to innovation, quality, sustainability, and developing talented people who want to build rewarding careers. We are currently seeking a motivated and ambitious **Junior Quantity Surveyor** to join our commercial team in Dublin. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or an early\-career Quantity Surveyor to gain hands on experience working on some of Ireland's most prestigious construction projects while learning from experienced commercial professionals. **The Role** ------------ Reporting to the Commercial Manager, Managing Quantity Surveyor, or Senior Quantity Surveyor, you will support the commercial management of projects throughout their lifecycle. You will gain exposure to procurement, subcontractor management, cost reporting, valuations, contract administration, and commercial controls while contributing to the successful delivery of major construction projects. **Key Responsibilities** ------------------------ ### **Procurement Tendering** * Assist with take\-offs and tender documentation. * Issue tender enquiries and track tender returns. * Review quotations against drawings, specifications, and scope requirements. * Prepare tender comparisons and identify commercial risks, exclusions, and scope gaps. * Support communication with subcontractors and suppliers throughout the procurement process. ### **Commercial Administration** * Assist in preparing monthly applications and valuations. * Support the assessment of subcontractor and consultant payment applications. * Assist with payment processing through SAP. * Maintain accurate commercial records and documentation. ### **Reporting Cost Control** * Support the preparation of monthly commercial reports, CVRs, cashflows, and project reviews. * Assist in monitoring project costs and forecasting financial performance. * Contribute to maintaining commercial procedures and reporting deadlines. ### **Contract Document Management** * Maintain commercial records and project documentation on SharePoint. * Support subcontractor appointment processes and ensure documentation is complete before work commences. * Assist with variation tracking and commercial correspondence. ### **Commercial Support** * Assist with value engineering initiatives and cost\-saving opportunities. * Support dispute resolution and claims preparation when required. * Provide estimating support during tender periods as needed. **What We're Looking For** -------------------------- ### **Essential** * Degree in Quantity Surveying, Construction Economics, or a related discipline. * Strong numerical and analytical skills. * Excellent attention to detail and organisational abilities. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office, particularly Excel. * Ability to work effectively as part of a team and manage multiple priorities. ### **Desirable** * Previous placement, internship, or work experience within the construction industry. * Knowledge of construction contracts and commercial processes. * Experience using SAP or similar commercial systems. **What We Offer** ----------------- * Competitive salary and benefits package. * Structured career development and mentoring. * Exposure to large\-scale, high\-profile construction projects. * Ongoing professional development and training opportunities. * Support towards achieving professional accreditation. * Opportunity to grow your career with one of Ireland's leading construction companies. If you are looking to build a successful career in quantity surveying with a company that values excellence, collaboration, and professional growth, we would love to hear from you. **Apply today and become part of the JJ Rhatigan team.**

title: Kitchen Assistant/Commis Chef
description: Job Description: Kitchen Porter / Commis Chef Gastro Pub Community‑Focused, We are a busy, welcoming gastro pub known for good food, warm service, and a strong sense of community. Our kitchen team takes pride in producing high‑quality dishes using fresh ingredients, and we’re looking for someone who wants to be part of that standard. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys a fast‑paced environment, values teamwork, and wants to grow their skills in a professional kitchen. Role Overview The Kitchen Porter / Commis Chef supports the smooth running of the kitchen by maintaining exceptional cleanliness and assisting with food preparation. Key Responsibilities Kitchen Hygiene \& Organisation Keep all kitchen areas clean, tidy, and safe throughout serviceWash pots, pans, utensils, and crockery quickly and efficiently Maintain cleaning schedules and uphold HACCP standards Assist with waste disposal, recycling, and general kitchen organisation Ensure equipment and storage areas are sanitised and well maintained Food Preparation \& Service Support * Assist chefs with basic prep: chopping, peeling, portioning, and plating * Help prepare ingredients for daily service and specials * Follow recipes and instructions with accuracy and care * Support stock rotation, labelling, and correct food storage * Maintain high standards of presentation and consistency in every dish Teamwork \& Communication * Work closely with the kitchen team to ensure smooth, efficient service * Communicate clearly and respectfully with chefs and front‑of‑house staff Show initiative, reliability, and a willingness to learn Contribute to a positive, supportive kitchen culture What We’re Looking For Strong work ethic and ability to stay calm under pressure * Good attention to detail and pride in maintaining high standards * Positive attitude and eagerness to develop culinary skills * Ability to follow direction and work well within a team * Commitment to cleanliness, safety, and consistency Experience * Previous kitchen experience is helpful but not essential * Full training provided for the right candidate What We Offer * A friendly, supportive team environment * Opportunities to learn and progress within the kitchen * Staff meals and in‑house benefits * Flexible hours depending on business needs * A chance to be part of a respected local gastro pub with a strong community Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €14\.00\-€16\.00 per hour Benefits: * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay Work Location: In person

title: L3 Infrastructure Engineer
description: This isn't a heads\-down ticket\-closing role. We need someone who can run complex infrastructure projects end\-to\-end, manage major incidents with calm authority, and explain technical decisions clearly to a non\-technical senior leadership team. If you've been the person your team turns to when things get hard, this is the role for you. **What you'll own** * **Change management** Own the full CAB process. Coordinate vendors and stakeholders, present changes in plain English to a non\-technical SMT, and produce clear post\-change documentation every time. * **P1 / major incident management** Lead major incidents from detection to resolution. Chase vendors, keep leadership informed at every step, and write up root cause analysis reports to close out properly * **Infrastructure project delivery** Take projects from design to go\-live. You won't be handed a single piece of someone else's plan — you'll own the whole thing and coordinate everyone around it * **Platform ownership** VMware vSphere (independent builds and upgrades), Veeam backup and DR, Windows Server 2016–2022, Azure / M365, and firewall management across the estate **What we're looking for** * 4\+ years in a corporate infrastructure environment with demonstrable project leadership * Hands\-on VMware vSphere: not just certification awareness, real build and upgrade ownership * Veeam backup and DR: configured and managed independently * Strong Windows Server (2016–2022\), Azure / M365, and firewall management experience * Compliance framework awareness: ISO 27001, SOX, NIST * ITIL\-aligned thinking: especially around change and incident management * Clear, confident communicator who can talk to both a NOC engineer and a CFO without changing what they're saying, just how they say it * Financial services or MSP background

title: L3 Infrastructure Engineer | Enterprise Platforms & Projects
description: **L3 Infrastructure Engineer \| Enterprise Platforms \& Projects** * True L3 ownership, not escalation\-only * Project leadership is built into the role * Enterprise\-grade technology with hands\-on responsibility An L3 role with real authority, real projects, and real impact.Enterprise\-scale environment with strong governance and modern tooling. A trusted senior position inside a values\-led, regulated organisation. Infrastructure environments do not become complex because engineers are careless. They become complex because scale, regulation, uptime expectations, and legacy all collide. This role exists to bring clarity, ownership, and senior technical leadership into that reality. The organisation operates at scale, across multiple sites, within a highly regulated environment, which means platforms must be resilient, auditable, secure, and well run. This is not a “keep the lights on” role. It is a senior engineering position where you are trusted to own problems end\-to\-end and leave the environment better than you found it. You will sit at Level 3, the escalation point for complex server, virtualisation, and infrastructure issues, but just as importantly, you will lead infrastructure projects, drive improvements, and help shape standards across the wider technology estate. You are not stepping into a rigid or over\-engineered IT function. This is a busy, hands\-on environment where ITIL, change control, and audit requirements exist for good reason, but where senior engineers are given autonomy, trust, and visibility. **What you will be responsible for** * Acting as the Level 3 escalation point for server and infrastructure issues across multiple sites * Owning complex incidents through root cause, resolution, and prevention * Leading infrastructure projects from design through to delivery * Administering and optimising: * Windows Server 2016 / 2019 / 2022 * Active Directory (DNS, DHCP, GPO, patching) * VMware (v8\.x) * Enterprise server and storage platforms * O365, Azure AD, Exchange (including ActiveSync) * Backup and recovery platforms * Networking (TCP/IP, DNS, routing, NAT, VPNs, LAN/WAN) * Supporting cloud usage for off\-site backup and disaster recovery * Ensuring strong documentation, configuration management, and change control * Supporting internal and external audits (ISO27001, SOX, NIST, finance, and client audits) * Mentoring and supporting junior engineers, raising overall team capability * Working closely with IT operations leadership and wider business stakeholders **What you will bring** * Strong hands\-on experience in an L3 infrastructure or senior support role * Deep troubleshooting capability across Windows, virtualisation, and networking * Experience operating in regulated, enterprise environments * A proactive mindset — you look for improvements, not just fixes * The ability to work independently while contributing strongly to a team * Excellent communication skills with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Relevant industry certifications (Microsoft, VMware) are beneficial * A degree in Computer Science or a related technical discipline is preferred **What is on offer** * Competitive salary and benefits package * Hybrid working model (Dublin\-based) * A stable, long\-term role within a well\-established organisation **Interested?** For more information or a confidential discussion, contact Samer Jaffer on \+353 1 649 8502 or samer.jaffer@archer.ie.

title: Linen Porter
description: You will receive training through our Dalata Academy to help you learn important skills. You will also meet experienced team members who can help you grow. At Dalata, we believe in promoting from within. If you work hard, you can have new opportunities in the future. Join us and be part of a friendly team! **Key Duties And Responsibilities** * Supplying linen and guest supplies to all Accommodation Assistants * Carrying out regular stocktakes of linen and guest supplies * Ensuring the upkeep and cleanliness of public areas and the linen room * Always providing excellent customer service to guests **Requirements** * Experience working with customers * An excellent command of the English language * Excellent organisational skills * Experience working in a fast\-paced environment * Be a strong team player **Benefits** * Free employee meals on duty * 50% Off F\&B in all properties * €/£60 employee rate on rooms for all Dalata\-owned hotels * 30% off the Friends \& Family rate * Extra AL day for every year worked (5 days to a maximum of 5 days) * Employee Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme/Transport Schemes * Pension access (Ask your HR Manager for local pension information) * Development Opportunities through our Dalata Academy\- support your career journey * Staff Appreciation Initiatives, fun team\-building activities and regular charity events **About Us** Dalata Hotel Group – We bring *the heart of hospitality* to every hotel we own and operate. We see our growth and expansion plans as a way of spreading our unique way of caring for our guests further around the world, bringing better experiences to more people. Currently, the Dalata difference can be felt at each of our hotels in Ireland, the UK, Germany \& the Netherlands, and we’ll keep expanding this further, focusing on key UK cities, Dublin and Continental Europe. Helping our people to grow and develop their careers is vitally important to us and is one of our highest priorities. We have many exciting and unique internal development programmes, each crafted to make sure our employees get the learning they need to be able to take the next step in their careers. Dalata Hotel Group Plc is an Inclusive Employer. We actively encourage people from all backgrounds and abilities to apply to be part of our team. title: MERCHANDISER - ROI
description: LEARN MORE ABOUT US AND THE ROLE:**Location:** Knocklyon, Dublin (D16 P6EK, A96 P2R9\) **Hours:** 2 hours per week **Days:** Monday **Benefits:** Holiday Entitlement, Merchandiser Recognition Programme, Employee Benefits Scheme, Expenses/Mileage/Travel Time where applicable As an inclusive employer Carlton Greetings are passionate about creating a workforce that represents the diversity of our communities and the customers we supply. WHO WE ARE At Carlton Greetings we are passionate about creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity.
