title: Pastry Chef de Partie
description: At Dean Group, we reimagine hospitality with character, not conformity. Founded in Dublin and now growing across Europe and the US, we create hotels shaped by personality, culture, and a heart\-led hustle. Each of our hotels, restaurants, bars and gyms tells its own story, brought to life through humble hospitality and genuine human connection. Our people are at the centre of it all. They show up with individuality, warmth and curiosity, shaping who we are and how guests experience our hotels every day. We welcome fresh thinking and imagination, because playing it safe never made anything unforgettable. We are now seeking an experienced, passionate **Pastry Chef de Partie** to join the team in **Jean\-Georges at The Leinster Hotel.** This is a rare opportunity to be part of an internationally celebrated brand, within an energetic, multicultural brigade of professionals who thrive on creativity and precision. Perched atop Dublin's most exciting Art Deco landmark, The Leinster, Jean\-Georges is a Michelin\-commended, modern fusion restaurant that celebrates the best of Irish ingredients elevated with bold, imaginative techniques. Part of the world\-renowned Jean\-Georges collection \- from the two\-Michelin\-star flagship in New York to restaurants in Kyoto, London, Las Vegas and beyond \- our Dublin home is at the forefront of Ireland's culinary scene. **Key Areas** * Support the Head Pastry in creating innovative, high\-quality desserts and pastries * Oversee daily operations in the pastry section of the kitchen * Deliver consistent presentation and flavour across all pastry items * Contribute to menu development and bespoke creations for special occasions * Maintain food safety, hygiene, and quality standards within the pastry section * Supervise and guide Pastry Commis **Job Purpose** The Pastry Chef de Partie plays a central role in delivering exceptional pastry and dessert offerings at our premium hotel. This role combines creativity, technical expertise, and leadership to produce high\-quality confections that meet both guest expectations and brand standards. The Pastry Chef de Partie oversees day\-to\-day pastry operations, and ensures that the section runs smoothly, efficiently, and in compliance with all food safety regulations. **Role Responsibilities** **Pastry Production \& Presentation** * Prepare and produce a variety of pastries, desserts, and confections to the highest standards * Decorate and present pastries using artistic techniques, icings, and toppings * Create custom\-made desserts and pastries for client requests, weddings, and events **Kitchen Operations \& Stock Management** * Prepare ingredients and operate baking equipment efficiently and safely * Monitor and manage stock of pastry ingredients and equipment * Assist with ordering supplies and ensuring the pastry section is adequately stocked * Maintain cleanliness, organisation, and readiness of the pastry section at all times **Quality Control \& Compliance** * Ensure all dishes are prepared in line with food hygiene and safety standards * Conduct regular checks to maintain consistency in quality, flavour, and presentation * Uphold brand and hotel standards across all pastry offerings **Team Leadership \& Collaboration** * Supervise and train Commis Pastry providing clear guidance and feedback * Work collaboratively with the Executive Chef, Sous Chefs, and Head Pastry to support smooth kitchen operations * Foster a positive, professional, and team\-oriented working environment **Preferred Experience \& Skills** * Extensive knowledge of pastry\-making and baking techniques * Strong artistic and creative skills in dessert decoration and presentation * In\-depth knowledge of sanitation principles and food preparation standards * Experience supervising or training junior kitchen staff * Familiarity with professional baking and kitchen equipment * Excellent communication skills and enthusiasm for teamwork * Passion for pastry and a desire to innovate within the role **Benefits:** * Global discounted staff room rates across the group * 50% off Food and 25% Beverage across the group * Friends and Family discount * Gym Membership Discounts\* * Continual training and development * Career progression within the Group * Gratuities \* * Meals on shift * Bike to work scheme * Taxsaver for travel * Refer a friend scheme * Wellness at work events and access to wellness platform * Employment Assistance Programme * Group discounts for cinema tickets, shopping deals etc * Team appreciation days * Regular funfilled activities and gatherings for all teams across the group * Applicable to certain positions Dean Group owns and operates a number of hotel across Ireland, in certain instances we may feel your skills and experience may suit another role we have available. By submitting your application, you are permitting Dean Group to contact you for other opportunities within the Group \#HP

title: Payments - EMEA Subsidiary Payments Sales Associate - Dublin
description: **JOB DESCRIPTION** The EMEA Subsidiary Payments Sales (SPS) team is responsible for covering Regional Treasury Centres and subsidiaries based in Europe, the Middle East and Africa (EMEA) of J.P. Morgan's multinational client relationships. The EMEA SPS team is closely aligned with the Global Corporate Bank and various other Banking Product verticals in delivering innovative payments solutions to J.P. Morgan's clients. This includes marketing the firm's in\-country capabilities as well as growing cross\-border revenue. As an EMEA Subsidiary Payments Sales Associate (SPS) within the EMEA Subsidiary Payments Sales (SPS) team, you will work in close day\-to\-day partnership with the Ireland Subsidiary Payments Sales lead, supporting a portfolio of multinational clients with complex regional and cross\-border treasury needs. You will gain exposure to client coverage, internal stakeholder management, commercial pipeline discipline and execution of client priorities, while also being connected to the broader EMEA SPS analyst and associate community for training, knowledge\-sharing and development. This is a high\-ownership role for someone who wants to take real responsibility for client follow\-through, internal execution and portfolio discipline, while developing quickly within J.P. Morgan's Payments franchise. The role offers strong on\-the\-job learning, regular coaching and connectivity with regional teams, including potential travel for training, team engagement and internal or client meetings. The role is well suited to someone looking to build a career in Payments Sales, Client Coverage or broader transaction banking, with direct exposure to clients, senior internal stakeholders and the practical delivery of solutions across the bank. **Job Responsibilities** * Work in close partnership with the Ireland Subsidiary Payments Sales lead to support day\-to\-day client coverage, portfolio execution, internal follow\-ups, meeting preparation and post\-meeting action tracking. * Own and maintain a structured tracker of client priorities, open items, internal follow\-ups, owners, timelines and next steps across the Ireland SPS portfolio \& be a reliable point of continuity for clients and internal partners, ensuring follow\-ups are tracked, progressed and closed out with a clear answer or next step. * Participate in client meetings and routine follow\-up calls where appropriate, building confidence in client communication, issue coordination and the practical delivery of Payments solutions. * Produce high\-quality call reports, meeting notes and post\-meeting follow\-ups with clear action items, owners and deadlines. * Proactively coordinate with internal partners across Payments, Banking, Product, Implementation, Service, Operations, Credit and KYC to help progress client requests, remove execution blockers and navigate a complex matrix organisation with professionalism and good judgement. * Coordinate new client onboardings, KYC processes and implementation activity to ensure a smooth client experience and timely execution of new business \& support pipeline management and revenue discipline, including maintaining accurate opportunity updates, next steps, expected timelines and key dependencies. * Assist with Request for Proposal (RFP) and Request for Information (RFI) responses, coordinating with internal teams to complete ad\-hoc analysis including cost/benefit analysis, pricing and profitability models. * Review and analyse portfolio data to identify key trends, volume fluctuations or risk issues, and provide clear summaries with actionable recommendations to the team lead. * Develop and maintain a working knowledge of JPMorgan Payments products, pricing philosophy and billing processes, building this understanding progressively through on\-the\-job exposure and training. * Stay informed on industry developments, market themes and competitor positioning relevant to the Irish and broader EMEA client base, and bring insights to client discussions and internal planning \& Bring problems together with proposed solutions or recommended next steps, using judgement to escalate matters appropriately where timelines, ownership or client outcomes are at risk. * Engage with the broader EMEA SPS analyst and associate community, including regional training, knowledge\-sharing, team connectivity and occasional travel where required \& Use approved digital, data and AI tools responsibly to improve productivity, including research, drafting, synthesis, data organisation, meeting preparation and follow\-up discipline. **Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills** * Prior experience in corporate banking, transaction banking, payments, treasury services, implementation, client service, relationship management support or a similar professional services role. We value attitude, aptitude and pace of learning, alongside relevant experience. * Strong ownership mindset with the ability to independently manage follow\-ups, track open items and drive tasks through to completion. * Highly organised, detail\-oriented and reliable, with the ability to manage multiple competing priorities without losing sight of deadlines or client outcomes. * Proactive working style, with a natural tendency to look for ways to help, anticipate needs and improve processes. * Strong written communication skills, with the ability to produce clear, concise and well\-structured updates, meeting notes, briefing materials and client follow\-ups. * Comfortable operating in a complex matrix organisation and following up with stakeholders across countries, functions and seniority levels. * Fluency in English is essential. **Preferred Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills** * Curious, coachable and fast\-learning, with a genuine interest in corporate treasury, payments, multinational clients and commercial banking. * Comfortable working closely with a senior sales lead in a fast\-moving environment, with the confidence to ask questions, take feedback, anticipate needs and build increasing ownership over time. * Collaborative, resilient and approachable, with the ability to build trust across teams and remain composed when priorities, timelines or conversations are challenging. **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world's most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants' and employees' religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **ABOUT THE TEAM** J.P. Morgan's Commercial \& Investment Bank is a global leader across banking, markets, securities services and payments. Corporations, governments and institutions throughout the world entrust us with their business in more than 100 countries. The Commercial \& Investment Bank provides strategic advice, raises capital, manages risk and extends liquidity in markets around the world.

title: Pharmacy Technician - Coolock
description: We currently have an exciting opportunity for a **Pharmacy Technician** to join the team in our **Hickey's Northside pharmacy in Coolock.** This is a **40\-hour (5\-day) permanent** position with the store's opening hours operating between **9 a.m. to 6:30 p.m. Monday to Thursday, 9 a.m. to 7p.m. on Friday, 9 a.m. to 6p.m.on Saturday and closed on Sundays.** The team currently comprises a **Retail Store Manager, a Supervising Pharmacist, 1 Support, 1 Relief, and 6 Technicians.** - **We Can Offer You** * Competitive Hourly rates * Great Colleague Discount * Bike to work scheme * Uniform provided **The Responsibilities Of The Role** * Be accurate with excellent attention to detail. * To prepare and dispense medicinal products under the supervision of the Pharmacist in a caring and professional manner. * Controlling stock levels and ordering medicines as determined by store requirements. * Preparation of blister packs and processing and tracking claims. * Reporting to and supporting the Pharmacist in a busy dispensary. **Requirements** * Experience working in a busy dispensary is an advantage, but newly qualified technicians are welcome to apply. * Experience with McLernons MPS (an advantage). * IPU Pharmacy Technician course or Higher Certificate in Pharmacy Technician Studies an advantage ***If you would be interested in being considered for current or future vacancies within a dispensary role, please apply or declare your interest to our Talent Acquisition Specialist Danielle Gillen at DGillen@uniphar.ie***

title: Planning Graduate - 2026 Suir Engineering Graduate
description: We have an exciting opportunity for Planning Graduate to join our 18\-month graduate programme. Here in Suir Engineering we are a leading international provider of innovative Mechanical, Electrical and Instrumentation Engineering Solutions. Our core sectors are Energy, Power and Renewables, Pharma, Food \& Beverage, and Data Centres. We provide best in class contracting solutions with in\-house, flexible resources capable of delivering a personable and consistent service to our customers. Over the past 40 years we have delivered projects for Irish and multinational clients across the globe. We are currently expanding significantly and are delivering large scale projects across Ireland, the U.K, Germany, Denmark \& Sweden. **We Have Opportunities To Join Our Programme In** * Dublin Office * Waterford Office Travel will be required various sites as requested. **Our Graduate Programme** In addition to on the job experience we also provide some specialized programmes for our Graduate Trainees * Soft Skills Modules – communications / presentation skills * Career Pathway Conversations – learn how different parts of our business work * Group Projects \& Presentations * Site Visits **Is this Graduate Program for me?** * Level 8 or relevant bachelor’s degree in relevant field * Analytical and problem\-solving skills with attention to detail. * A proactive attitude with a willingness to learn and take on challenges. * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Flexibility to work on different sites and relocate if required. **What will I be doing?** * Continuously model the steps of the construction project in different diagrams. Monitor and report on project progress. * Make decisions related to logistics and resources. * Monitor \& Report on Internal productivity and issue Earned Value Analysis on a weekly basis. * Attend meetings with the Project Team to discuss progress made and any issues that arise over the course of the project. * Any other reasonable and relevant duties as requested by your manager, necessary to meet the ongoing needs of the company. * Daily \& weekly programme updates with the client. **In Return From Suir Engineering** * Salary starting from €40,000 per year * 23 days annual leave * Company Pension contributions \- no waiting periods * Paid Maternity and Paternity Leave * Private Health Insurance contribution * Life and Disability cover * EAP for employees and their families * Educational Assistance, CPD, Chartership supports and upskilling opportunities * Opportunities for international assignments to work on one of our European projects in Sweden, Denmark, or Germany. Our way of working is called the Suir Way, designed to help us on a journey of continuous improvement. * Our people are empowered to make changes to ensure a quality install through effective ways of working. * Our people have a voice and feel empowered. * We have an in\-built culture of innovation, learning and continuous improvement. * Value is delivered every step of the journey. Our efficient processes deliver value. * We have a standard, consistent and fully integrated health, safety, environment, and quality system within the business. Suir Engineering are an equal opportunities employer; we value our greatest asset …. our People. We do not require the support of Recruitment agencies at this time.

title: Pre Tendering Coordinator
description: **Role:** Pre\-Tendering Coordinator (Civil Engineering) **Country:** Republic of Ireland **Location:** Sallins, Co. Kildare **About Wills Bros Ltd** Wills Bros is seeking an experienced a **Pre\-Tendering Coordinator (Civil Engineering)** to join our Precontracts team at our Sallins office. This is a unique opportunity to play a key role in a live, high\-security environment, contributing to the successful delivery of complex structural and civil works while upholding the highest standards of quality, safety, and compliance. ***This is a full\-time, office\-based role in our Sallins office.*** **Role Overview** Under the oversight of the Lead Prequalification Manager, the Assistant Prequalification Manager will lead the preparation of select **Pre** ‑ **Qualification Questionnaires (PQQs), Expressions of Interest (EOIs)** and selection stage submissions for public and private sector civil engineering projects. The role involves close collaboration with Engineering, Commercial, Quality, HSEQ, Technical Bids and Site teams and requires strong technical writing, organisation and commercial awareness. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead and coordinate PQQ, EOI and selection stage submissions, managing inputs, timelines and reviews * Interpret procurement documentation and scoring methodologies to ensure full compliance * Draft clear, evidence\-based responses that reflect Wills Bros’ experience, capability and differentiators * Manage and maintain ISO, HSEQ and sustainability content, ensuring it is current and aligned to client requirements * Maintain project experience records, CVs and evidence libraries to support repeatable, high\-quality submissions * Coordinate inputs from project, operational and functional teams to meet deadlines and quality standards * Manage procurement portals and tender databases, tracking opportunities and submission status * Support and mentor junior staff involved in prequalification submissions **Qualifications \& Experience** * Relevant third level qualification in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, Project Management, Quality, Health \& Safety, Business, Marketing or a related discipline * Construction/civil engineering experience within the Irish and/or EU market * Demonstrable experience in prequalification, pre‑contracts, bidding or tendering * Excellent document production skills, with the ability to simplify complex information and present it clearly * Strong written communication, organisation and attention to detail * Working knowledge of Irish public procurement and tender processes **What We Offer** • 24 days’ annual leave plus bank holidays. • Employee Assistance Programme. • Death in Service benefit (4x basic salary). • Pension scheme. • Income Protection. • Digital Doctor. • Bike to Work scheme. • Company\-funded professional development, including courses, certifications, and memberships in accredited bodies such as Engineers Ireland. • Ongoing professional development and career progression opportunities. • A supportive, collaborative working environment. • The chance to contribute to high\-profile, impactful projects. If you are a motivated Pre\-Tendering Coordinator looking to contribute to a high\-profile infrastructure project and grow your career with a leading tier 1 main contractor, we would be delighted to hear from you. Please note: We are not accepting applications from recruitment agencies at this time. How to Apply: Apply now or contact Paula Murphy for a confidential discussion. Alternatively, email your CV to hr@willsbros.com

title: Pricing Analyst
description: Barden is delighted to have been engaged by our client to identify an Pricing Analyst to join their team on a full\-time permanent basis. The hiring manager is seeking a commercially minded finance professional with expertise in financial modelling, bid support, and stakeholder partnering, capable of delivering accurate, data\-driven commercial solutions. **ABOUT THE ROLE:** * Develop detailed cost models and pricing solutions for tenders and bids ensuring commercial viability and alignment with client requirements. * Partner with Sales, Operations, Procurement, and Finance teams to translate service specifications into deliverable and cost\-effective solutions. * Perform financial modelling, sensitivity analysis, and scenario planning to assess key assumptions, risks, and pricing strategies. * Support the preparation of business cases, commercial submissions, and bid responses, often working to challenging deadlines. * Validate cost assumptions and delivery models to ensure accurate, competitive, and robust pricing proposals. * Drive continuous improvement of pricing methodologies, tools, and processes while monitoring market trends and industry best practices. **ABOUT THE PERSON:** * Experienced commercial finance professional with a background in pricing, bid support, and cost modelling. * Strong track record of supporting complex tenders. * Skilled in building detailed cost models. * Advanced Excel user with expertise in financial modelling and pricing analysis. * Effective collaborator, partnering with operational stakeholders to design practical and cost\-effective delivery models. * Commercially focused, with the ability to balance customer value, profitability, and risk.

title: Private Equity Investment Associate
description: **Job Title:** Private Equity Investment Associate **Sector:** Private Equity **Location:** Dublin **Salary:** DOE plus bonus and benefits **Our Client** Our client is a highly regarded Private Equity firm with an exceptional track record of partnering with ambitious businesses and supporting them through periods of growth and transformation. Following continued expansion and a strong pipeline of transactions, they are seeking to appoint a Private Equity Investment Associate to join their high\-calibre investment team. **Why should you apply?** This is a rare opportunity to join one of the most respected investment platforms in the Irish market. Working alongside experienced investors and entrepreneurs, you will gain exposure across the full investment lifecycle including origination, execution, portfolio management and value creation initiatives. The role offers outstanding exposure to senior decision\-makers, management teams and investment committees while providing an exceptional platform for long\-term career development. You will play a meaningful role in evaluating investment opportunities and helping portfolio companies deliver strategic and operational improvements. **Who should apply?** This opportunity is ideally suited to ambitious finance professionals who have excelled in demanding and high\-performing environments and are looking to build a long\-term career in private equity. Experience in the following areas would be beneficial: • 3\-6 years' experience within Transaction Services, Corporate Finance, M\&A Advisory, Deals Advisory, Investment Banking or Private Equity • Experience gained within a leading professional services firm, advisory platform, investment bank or investment environment with exposure to complex transactions • Strong financial modelling, valuation and analytical capabilities • An excellent academic background combined with a consistent track record of achievement and progression • Commercially minded with strong problem\-solving skills and the ability to think strategically • Highly motivated with strong attention to detail and the ability to operate effectively in a fast\-paced and intellectually rigorous environment • Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to engage confidently with senior stakeholders, management teams and external advisers **Role and Reporting Lines** Reporting directly to the Investment Director and working closely with Partners and the wider investment team, responsibilities will include: • Investment Evaluation – Analyse potential investment opportunities through detailed financial, commercial and strategic assessment. • Financial Modelling – Develop and maintain sophisticated valuation and financial models to support investment decisions. • Transaction Execution – Support the execution of acquisitions, refinancing processes and exit transactions. • Due Diligence Management – Coordinate financial, commercial, operational and legal due diligence processes with external advisers. • Portfolio Value Creation – Partner with management teams to monitor performance and support strategic initiatives. • Market Research – Identify industry trends, competitive dynamics and emerging investment opportunities. • Stakeholder Management – Develop relationships with management teams, advisers, lenders and key market participants. • Investment Committee Support – Prepare investment papers, presentations and materials to support internal decision\-making processes. **Interested in this position?** To apply please submit your CV to Lisa Tully who is managing this assignment via the link below or to inquire further please contact us directly on 01 529 4200\.

title: Process Operator, Rendering (Shift Work)
description: College Group was founded in 1989 and currently operates from three state\-of\-the\-art sites; Nobber in Meath, Monnery in Cavan and Newry in Down. We are currently seeking to recruit an enthusiastic **Process Operator** to join the Production Team in our Rendering Plant based in Nobber. This position will be an integral part of our operation and will work to maximize efficiency and support delivery of key performance indicators. The role will operate on an 8 hour shift pattern rostered over days and nights which will include weekends. **Roles And Responsibilities** * Operate the production PLC system to control production, gather data and generate reports * Identify and report any problems to the production supervisor * Achieve process capacity and KPIs * Hand\-over of key information at shift transitions * Sample and deliver products to the lab for QC * Perform general facility maintenance and house\-keeping including equipment inspection and preventative maintenance * Follow all safety guidelines and rules on\-site regulated by the HSA and other regulatory authorities **Experience Required** * Ability to organize and prioritize work for timely completion * Excellent computer skills * Strong attention to detail * Effective communication skills

title: Product Owner
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About The Role** An exciting opportunity has opened up for a Product Owner to join our team at Davy. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Define and communicate the product vision for the squad, aligning to business strategy * Own and manage the backlog, prioritised against value outcomes and stakeholder requirements, and lead refinement, sprint planning and review sessions for their squad * Translate business requirements into features and detailed user stories with acceptance criteria * Drive roadmap planning across release horizons and quarterly planning cycles * Act as primary liaison between business and technology, and wider stakeholders as required, including reporting and wider updates * Execute with Agile ways of working and methodologies * Manage the governance and risk agenda for the squad appropriately, adhering to compliance and regulatory standards, participating in governance forums as required and maintain an appropriate audit trail **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: Experience \& Background * 5\+ experience as a Product Owner / Product Manager in technology\-driven environments * Strong track record delivering digital products and transformation initiatives across industries (e.g., financial services, eCommerce, or similar) * Experience working across global, multi\-stakeholder environments with distributed teams * Background in end\-to\-end product delivery, from concept through to launch and optimisation * Previous experience as a Business Analyst or similar role is highly beneficial Product Ownership \& Delivery * Demonstrated ability to define and execute product strategy and roadmap aligned to business goals * Expertise in managing and prioritising a product backlog, ensuring clear, actionable user stories and acceptance criteria * Experience delivering complex digital products, including API integrations, data transformation, and automation initiatives * Strong understanding of Agile delivery (Scrum / SAFe) with hands\-on involvement in key ceremonies (planning, refinement, demos, retrospectives) * Ability to define and track KPIs and success metrics to measure product performance Stakeholder Management * Excellent ability to engage, influence, and collaborate with senior stakeholders, including business, technology, and operations * Experience running workshops to gather requirements and solve business problems * Skilled at balancing customer needs, business priorities, and technical constraints Leadership \& Collaboration * Experience leading and motivating cross\-functional Agile teams (engineering, UX, business analysts, etc.) * Strong team leadership and mentoring capabilities, with the ability to support and guide junior team members * Ability to manage dependencies, risks, and delivery timelines in complex environments Technical \& Analytical Skills * Strong understanding of technology concepts, including APIs, data integration, and digital platforms * Comfortable working closely with engineering teams on solution design and delivery * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to translate data into actionable insights * Experience driving process improvements and operational efficiency initiatives Tools \& Ways of Working * Proficient in tools such as Jira, Confluence, and other Agile delivery platforms * Experience with process mapping, documentation, and workflow tools * Familiarity with data tools (e.g., SQL) or technical concepts is advantageous **What We Offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. Health and Wellbeing Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. Reward and Recognition We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. Growth and Development Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. Positive Workplace Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. Values and Culture Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Production Operative
description: The Colas Group operates internationally in all aspects of transport infrastructure. As part of the global organisation, Colas Ireland is primarily involved the distribution of bitumen products, emulsion and emulsifier manufacturing, and the provision of specialist road maintenance services on the national, regional and local road network. In Ireland Colas operates from 14 locations across the island of Ireland. **Purpose of the Role** The purpose of this role is to run the day\-to\-day operations in our manufacturing facility in Dublin. The role would suit applicants from any industrial, production or warehousing environment who wish to be trained, developed and progressed into a worldwide company offering stability and job security. The salary for this role is €18\.36 per hour plus overtime and excellent benefits listed below. Applications from D12 and surrounding areas would be preferred. **Main Responsibilities** In this role you’ll be trained and supported in running our automated and manual production systems, setting up and maintaining equipment, monitoring quality and output, and handling raw material requests and reporting. You’ll load, package and ship products as needed, safely offload bitumen, and report any faults, incidents or near misses to the maintenance or safety team. Safety and good housekeeping are essential: follow SOPs, wear PPE, take part in drills and safety meetings, and help maintain a tidy workplace. You’ll also help train new starters, suggest cost\-saving ideas, meet production targets, and provide excellent customer service. We’ll provide friendly, hands\-on onboarding, ongoing training and daily support from an experienced team so you can succeed and grow. Other reasonable duties may be required. **Ideal Candidate** The ideal candidate will have experience in an industrial or manual environment or have the desire and willingness to learn and develop into a production operative role. You will demonstrate high ethical standards, reliability and adherence to our health and safety procedures. **Package Description** Here at Colas Group, we offer a great total compensation package of €18\.36 per hour plus overtime , including: * Contibutory Pension Scheme * Life Assurance Scheme / Death in Service x3 * Safety Award Bonus * Sick Pay \& Compassionate Leave * 21 days annual leave per year \+ Public Holidays and service days (one for every 5 years worked, capped at 20 years) * RediCare Health and Wellbeing Support * Employee Assistance Programme for employees, their spouse/partner and any dependents over the age of 16 years living at home. * Mental Health, Legal, Financial, Career and Child and Elderly Care Support * Free car parking. * Professional subscriptions – role related * Enhanced Maternity, Adoptive, and Paternity Benefit *At Colas, our culture is guided by four core values: **Respect** , **Commitment** , **Pioneering** , and **Sharing** . These values shape how we work, collaborate, and grow together. We believe that **respect** is the foundation of every relationship. By treating others as we wish to be treated, we foster fairness, transparency, and inclusion within our teams and with our clients, communities, and partners. **Commitment** means dedicating ourselves fully to everything we do. We strive to create an environment where everyone feels valued, supported, and empowered to make a meaningful impact. With a **pioneering** spirit, we prioritise bold innovation over blind ambition. We welcome new ideas, invest in our people, and work to deliver sustainable infrastructure solutions that shape the future. Through **sharing** , we build trust and solidarity, encouraging collaboration and growth at all levels of the business.* *Colas Group is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce that mirrors the communities we serve. As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from all backgrounds. If you want a workplace where your voice is valued and your potential is developed, Colas is the right choice.*

title: Programme Coordinator Hacketstown, Carlow
description: **Company Description** Sherpa Kids is a global organization dedicated to providing quality care for primary school children in their local communities. With a presence in approximately 265 schools worldwide, including 60 local services in Ireland now \- and growing since 2014, we strive to make a difference in the lives of over 9,000 children daily. **Job Description** In Ireland, we offer Before School, After School, and Holiday Clubs programmes tailored to meet the needs of national\-aged children. Operating on\-site at schools, our customized services feature fun, structured, and engaging daily activities. At Sherpa Kids, we believe our work goes beyond childcare. Our purpose is rooted in supporting and making a positive impact on Ireland's families. We are committed to helping them achieve their goals through the expert care we provide. * Programme Lead must have experience in managing and leading a team * Own car required for programme lead * Maintain the day\-to\-day activities * Interacting with the children, their families and other staff members each day. * Lots of energy and enthusiasm to work with children. * Experience working with school aged children is preferred (Minimum 2 year) * Proficiency in technology and software (Word, Outlook, Teams, etc.). **Qualifications*** Experience working with School Aged children (Minimum 1 Year) * Childcare qualification Fetac Level 5 of equivalent * Basic knowledge of child development * Proficiency in English, both verbal and written * Garda vetting is essential (completed by us after interview stage). * Work Permit/ VISA essential * At least 2 written references checks are required before starting **Additional Information** Monday\-Friday 7:50am\-8:50am and 1:15pm\-6pm \#INDM

title: Project Control Specialist
description: **Infrastructure Project Controls Specialists** (all levels), \- **Infrastructure \& Capital Projects** \- Business Consulting Location: **Dublin,** Ireland Contract Type: **Full\-Time – Permanent** **The team and the opportunity** To continue the successful growth of our client’s **Infrastructure \& Capital Projects** team within their large **Consulting** practice, are looking for experienced and motivated **Project Controls Specialists** with a background in delivering complex, **High‑Value Capital Programmes.** The successful candidate will be responsible for overseeing and managing all aspects of **Project Controls** within the **Infrastructure Sector** , ensuring projects are delivered on time, within budget, and in line with quality and governance requirements. This role requires meticulous attention to detail, strong analytical capability, and the ability to communicate and collaborate effectively with a broad range of stakeholders. Their **Infrastructure \& Capital Projects** team sits within the **Transformation Delivery** competency of **Irish Consulting practice.** Their team is committed to delivering exceptional client services across a range of sectors including **Energy, Utilities, Transport, Water, Gas, Telecoms, Housing, and Healthcare.** Our ambition is to become the **Trusted Advisor** for our clients in the **Procurement, Construction** , and **Operational Phases** of **Major Capital Programmes** by leveraging: Their people and their extensive industry experience. Existing partnerships and relationships. Their global experience across the **Infrastructure \& Capital** projects lifecycle. **Your key responsibilities** To support the continued growth of their business, they are currently recruiting **Project Controls Specialists** with an **Engineering, Technical** , or **Construction** ‑related background. Work directly with their **infrastructure clients** across our focus sectors ( **Energy, Water, Transport, Gas, Telecoms** , **Housing,** and **Healthcare** ) to develop innovative insights and solutions to meet their needs. Develop and implement **Project Control** processes and procedures to effectively manage **Scope, Schedule, Cost** , and **Quality.** Collaborate with **Project Managers, Engineers, Contractors** , and other stakeholders to establish project baselines and monitor performance against established metrics. Create and maintain detailed **Project Schedules** , including **Critical Path Analysis** and resource allocation, to optimise project timelines and identify potential risks and opportunities. **Monitor Project Costs** and expenditures, track budget variances, and provide timely analysis and reporting to support informed decision\-making. Identify and mitigate **Project Risks** through proactive risk assessment, analysis, and mitigation strategies. Lead or support regular progress meetings and presentations to update **Project Stakeholders** on project status, key milestones, and performance metrics. Utilise **Project Management Systems** and tools to enhance **Project Controls Processes** and enhance project visibility and transparency. **Provide Mentorship** and guidance to **Junior Project Controls** staff, fostering a culture of continuous improvement and professional development within the team. Support the preparation of **Project Proposals, Bids** , and **Cost Estimates** by providing accurate and reliable **Project Controls Data** and **Analysis.** Ensure compliance with relevant industry **Standards, Regulations** , and **Best Practices** in **Project Controls** and **Management.** **Internal Responsibilities** Support **Client Account Management** activities, including **Project Resourcing, Financial Management** , and **Quality and Risk Management.** Build and maintain strong relationships with clients and their colleagues to support business develop. Communicate effectively with **Senior Management** and support the development, management and motivation of high\-performing teams. Develop a strong understanding of their services offerings and proactively identify opportunities to enhance client value. Support team development activities, including: Coaching, and mentoring. Conducting performance reviews and contributing to feedback. Supporting recruitment, retention and training initiatives. Maintaining continuous technical and professional development. Understanding, applying, and communicating workplace policies and procedures **To qualify for the role, you must have:** 3rd level educational qualification in **Engineering, Construction** , or a related field. **Minimum of 5 years** of experience in **Project Controls** or **Project Management** within the **Infrastructure** sector, with a proven track record of **Supporting Or Managing** **Large\-Scale Programmes.** Ideal candidates will have:\- Experience in **Consulting** or similar client facing role. Multidisciplinary experience including **Project Management** and **Engineering Feasibility** and **Design.** **Excellent Stakeholder Management** and communication skills with a track record in **Managing Complex Programmes.** **Strong Analytical** and **Problem\-Solving skills** , with the ability to **Analyse Complex Data Sets** and develop actionable insights. **Excellent Communication** and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively collaborate with diverse stakeholders at all levels. **Demonstrated Leadership Capability** with a proactive, delivery‑focused approach. Knowledge of industry\-specific regulations, standards, and best practices in **Infrastructure Development** and **Construction.** Additional professional qualifications, such as **PRINCE2® or PMP** , are desirable.

title: Project Coordinator Specialist
description: **Project Coordination Specialist** **The Role:** A leading organisation in the renewable energy and infrastructure sector is seeking a Project Coordination Specialist to support the successful delivery of major infrastructure projects. Working closely with Project Managers and the PMO, this role will provide project management, reporting and coordination support to ensure projects are delivered on schedule, within budget and in accordance with governance requirements. **Key Responsibilities:** * Support the delivery of large\-scale infrastructure and energy projects. * Assist with the development and maintenance of project management plans covering scope, schedule, cost, quality, risk, procurement and stakeholder management. * Ensure project governance processes are followed and support reporting to senior management and key stakeholders. * Build effective working relationships across project teams and support knowledge sharing initiatives. * Coordinate project communications, meetings, reports and presentations. * Manage project documentation and document control systems, ensuring accurate revision control and distribution. * Track project actions, risks, issues and deliverables, ensuring timely follow\-up and resolution. * Prepare and maintain project controls documentation including programmes, risk registers, action logs and financial trackers. * Support stakeholder and community engagement activities where required. * Assist with procurement processes and contract administration activities. * Monitor project expenditure and prepare regular financial reports, budget updates and forecasts. * Support compliance with health, safety and organisational policies. **Requirements:** * Third\-level qualification in Engineering, Business, Project Management or a related discipline. * Postgraduate qualification in Project Management desirable. * Minimum 1 year's experience in a project coordination or project support role within infrastructure, engineering, construction or energy sectors. * Strong understanding of project management principles and processes. * Excellent communication, reporting and stakeholder engagement skills. * Strong organisational and administrative capabilities. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office and Microsoft Project. * Experience with document management and collaboration platforms such as SharePoint, ProjectWise or similar would be advantageous. * Professional project management certification (e.g. PMP or equivalent) desirable. * Knowledge of the Irish energy or infrastructure sector would be beneficial. **Benefits:** * Competitive remuneration package commensurate with experience. * Matched Pension Contribution. * Comprehensive Annual Leave entitlement. * Hybrid Working arrangements. * Free on\-site parking. * Opportunity to contribute to the delivery of major renewable energy and infrastructure projects. * Supportive team environment with opportunities for professional growth and development.

title: QA Automation Engineer
description: Can you bring your technical expertise to our QA team based in Dublin, where you can enjoy a collaborative culture and build a career you can be proud of? **What You Would Do As Our QA Automation Engineer** As our QA Automation Engineer, you will play a key role within the R\&D team, building our automated testing capability from the ground up across our cloud\-connected security platform, mobile applications, desktop software, and connected devices. This newly created position will focus on introducing scalable automation, improving regression efficiency, and increasing release confidence as the platform grows. You’ll work closely with software developers, firmware engineers, and product teams to develop practical, maintainable frameworks. You would also: * Build and maintain scalable automated testing frameworks across cloud, mobile, and desktop platforms to reduce manual testing. * Identify and prioritise high\-impact automation opportunities while balancing manual and automated testing approaches. * Automate key workflows such as authentication, device onboarding, syncing, firmware updates, and user management. * Support quality through manual/exploratory testing, defect troubleshooting with developers, and clear test documentation. * Strengthen QA processes by contributing to CI/CD integration, release testing, and long\-term automation strategy. **The Skills And Experience You Need** We are looking for someone who has: * Proven experience in QA/testing with strong knowledge of automated regression and test design principles. * Hands\-on experience building or maintaining automated test frameworks for cloud, mobile, or desktop applications. * Proficiency in API testing and scripting/programming (e.g. Python or JavaScript). * Strong troubleshooting, debugging, and analytical skills with the ability to work independently and take ownership. * Effective communication and collaboration skills, working closely with developers and product teams. **What We Offer** We’re passionate about providing amazing opportunities and benefits, so you can continue and progress a lifelong career with us – here’s what we have to offer: * Learning and career development opportunities, whether it’s online learning, management training or enhancing your skills. * A competitive salary and incentive schemes. * Variety of benefits including Holiday Flex, Cycle 2 Work, attractive company pension, discount portal, recognition scheme and wellbeing program… to name just a few! **We review applications regularly, so don’t wait** We are building diverse, inclusive teams, and encourage applications from everyone who can see themselves working with us . Just set up your profile and apply here. To make sure your personal data is safe, we don’t look at any applications sent by email or post. If you have any questions about the role or the process, email Nadia Aslam, Recruitment Business Partner at nadia.aslam@assaabloy.com . Let’s create a safer and more open world \- together! To find out more about us, visit www.assaabloy.com **We are the ASSA ABLOY Group** Our people have made us the global leader in access solutions. In return, we open doors for them wherever they go. With nearly 63,000 colleagues in more than 70 different countries, we help billions of people experience a more open world. Our innovations make all sorts of spaces – physical and virtual – safer, more secure, and easier to access. As an employer, we value results – not titles, or backgrounds. We empower our people to build their career around their aspirations and our ambitions – supporting them with regular feedback, training, and development opportunities. Our colleagues think broadly about where they can make the most impact, and we encourage them to grow their role locally, regionally, or even internationally. As we welcome new people on board, it’s important to us to have diverse, inclusive teams, and we value different perspectives and experiences.

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: At PJ Hegarty \& Sons, we pride ourselves in a holistic approach to Quantity Surveying. Our responsibility is to manage the company’s financial interests in a manner as wide as is possible and simultaneously assist in project delivery to meet the Client’s vision for the project. These responsibilities include the management of costs and value and also the management of our contractual relationships with the Client and the supply chain. We are currently seeking experienced Quantity Surveyors in the Greater Dublin area. You will be responsible for the cost and value management of projects from mobilisation to the settlement of final accounts. **Overview** As a Quantity Surveyor in our team, you will have progressed from graduate level and will be focused upon becoming a Senior Surveyor. We have a progressive training programme focused upon your development and tailored to meet the requirements of the SCSI/RICS Assessment of Professional Competence. You will be given the tools to assist you manage a trainee or graduate quantity surveyor. You will also participate in departmental training and will be involved in receiving training from seniors and also giving training to juniors. title: Rack Decomissioning Technician , DCC Communities, DECOM
description: **Description** The Decommissioning Technical team is responsible for processing and facilitating large scale legacy Data Center equipment through a liquidation process to ensure that targeted equipment is ready to be removed off premises. Team members will also process large volumes of customer data to ensure that all customer data is sanitized and properly managed. This position requires candidates to be a self\-starter, highly motivated individual who can adhere to strict processes and be able to perform repetitive tasks with no deviation and demonstrate continuously consistent results. Team Members will be working in a team environment not only with other members of the team but also from other teams and must be able to communicate and promote cross\-team functionality. This is an entry level position and qualified candidates do not require intimate details of software operations or networking protocols. Amazon Data Centers takes customer data with the utmost importance, and selected candidates must be able to perform repetitive tasks without variance and adhere to strict Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) that ensures customer data is protected and properly handled. Key job responsibilities **Data Center Technicians (Decommission Technician) Will Be Responsible For** Decommissioning and processing of legacy hardware out of the data center facilities Moving/lifting packages usually no greater than 75 lbs. Unpacking/repacking fragile electronics Relocating and moving of large enterprise equipment Creating/attaching labels and barcodes Hard drive installation and removal, and destruction Basic Networking removal/installation and clearing of configurations. Physical layer and Application layer Updating progress through a web ticketing system Reporting of processes and regular status updates Documenting procedures Escalating to senior technicians and management as needed/required **Familiarity With Basic PC Components/hardware** * HDD/SSD/Flash/Removable storage media * USB/FireWire/Esata * Ram/CPU/Motherboard/Input\-Output devices There may be an element of shift based work required with this role. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. title: Real Estate Solicitor | Permanent | Cork
description: After a sustained period of significant growth, the real estate team at Philip Lee LLP is looking to hire a 1\-3PQE solicitor. **The Firm** Philip Lee LLP is one of Ireland’s leading commercial law firms with offices in Dublin, Cork, London and New York. Specific initiatives to support our employees include but are not limited to: * associate and partner development programmes; * the opportunity to join various firm committees, including our social, wellness, green, pro bono and diversity committees; * subsidised yoga and fitness classes; and * numerous social events throughout the year. Please submit your CV in confidence to hoshaughnessy@philiplee.ie.

title: Recreational Coordinator
description: Cheeverstown is seeking an enthusiastic, creative and motivated Recreational Co\-Ordinator to join our team. This exciting role will support adults with intellectual disabilities to access meaningful opportunities, build relationships, participate in their communities, and enjoy fulfilling social, recreational and leisure experiences. Working across Respite and New Directions Services, you will play a key role in developing person\-centred programmes that promote inclusion, independence, active citizenship, human rights, and community participation. **About The Role** As Recreational Co\-Ordinator, you will lead and coordinate engaging activities and opportunities that reflect the interests, goals and aspirations of the people we support. You will work closely with individuals, families, multidisciplinary teams and community organisations to ensure meaningful participation and positive outcomes. This role is ideal for someone who is passionate about social inclusion, advocacy, community development, sport, recreation and person\-centred practice. Key Responsibilities Person\-Centred Support * Develop and coordinate meaningful recreational, social and community\-based activities. * Promote choice, independence and active participation. * Support individuals to achieve personal goals and develop new skills. * Actively advocate for the rights and inclusion of people supported by Cheeverstown. * Build strong relationships with families, community groups and local organisations. Community Engagement * Facilitate access to local community activities and events. * Coordinate social groups, friendship networks and community\-based opportunities. * Support participation in Special Olympics training and competitions. * Develop partnerships within local communities through networking and collaboration. * Identify opportunities to enhance social inclusion and community participation. Programme Development * Assist with planning and developing innovative recreational programmes. * Complete assessments and gather information to ensure supports are tailored to individual needs. * Work collaboratively with Respite and New Directions teams. * Support service development initiatives and partnership opportunities. **Essential Requirements** * Relevant qualification in Social Care, Health Care, Community Development, Recreation, Disability Studies or a related discipline. * Minimum 2 years' experience supporting people with intellectual disabilities. * Experience working collaboratively with individuals, families and multidisciplinary teams. * Strong organisational, planning and coordination skills. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to work independently and use initiative. * Strong IT skills, including Microsoft Office. * Full clean driving licence. * Commitment to person\-centred practice, human rights and community inclusion. Desirable * Experience coordinating community programmes, sports, social activities or events. * Knowledge of New Directions and person\-centred planning. * Experience supporting Special Olympics or similar recreational initiatives. * Experience in advocacy and community networking.

title: Regulatory Reporting Analyst
description: ***🚀 We’re on a mission to make money work for everyone.*** We’re waving goodbye to the complicated and confusing ways of traditional banking. After starting as a prepaid card, our product offering has grown a lot in the last 10 years in the UK. As well as personal and business bank accounts, we offer joint accounts, accounts for 16\-17 year olds, a free kids account and credit cards in the UK, with more exciting things to come beyond. Our UK customers can also save, invest and combine their pensions with us. With our hot coral cards and get\-paid\-early feature, combined with financial education on social media and our award winning customer service, we have a long history of creating magical moments for our customers! We’re not about selling products \- we want to solve problems and change lives through Monzo ❤️ 📍Dublin \| 💰€45,000 \- €55,000 \+ incentive awards tied to your performance➕ benefits \| Hear from the team ✨ **⭐Our Finance team** Our Finance team is growing and we’re looking for a curious, adaptive and data\-focused Regulatory Reporting Analyst with a strong attention to detail to join our regulatory reporting team at Monzo EU (Ireland). The team is responsible for understanding our prudential regulatory reporting requirements, working on projects to set up and submit these and collaborating with teams across the Group. **🔑You’ll play a key role by...** * Setting up and operating processes to produce timely, accurate and complete regulatory reporting and Pillar 3 disclosures for Monzo’s regulators (and the associated management information required for sign\-off) e.g. Central Bank of Ireland * Understanding, reviewing and documenting the applicable regulatory interpretations, such as the Capital Requirements Regulations (CRR) and Basel 3\.1 * Designing and implementing controls to ensure regulatory reporting is timely, accurate and complete and that regulatory compliance is maintained * Facilitating appropriate governance over regulatory reporting and ensuring compliance with the applicable control framework * Implementation and testing (UAT) of new releases of the Bank’s automated regulatory reporting tool * Partnering with teams internally to help them make informed business decisions and providing regulatory reporting input into new product launches * Ensuring any regulatory reporting you’re responsible for runs like clockwork, including leveraging data visualisation tools to build dashboards and facilitate useful insights * Designing brilliant ways to automate your work and leverage technology to ensure we scale with the business **🤩We’d love to hear from you if…** * You’re a qualified or part qualified accountant with experience working within financial services. * You have previous knowledge of regulatory reporting, such as capital reporting (COREP), liquidity reporting (e.g. LCR) or financial reporting (FINREP) * You’re excited by data and have a sharp eye for detail, with amazing Excel or GSheets skills * You have experience with some coding languages like SQL would be a bonus, although a willingness to learn is just fine * You have a proven record of implementing processes to deliver to non\-negotiable, regulatory deadlines * You have experience in the large\-scale implementation and testing (UAT) of automated reporting solutions * You want to automate or enhance recurring processes, particularly by leveraging technology, but know how to balance this with getting the actual reports out! * You have an open mind, enjoy transparency and think outside the box * What we’re doing at Monzo excites you! Not ticking every box? That’s totally okay! Studies show that women and people of colour might hesitate to apply unless they meet every single requirement. At Monzo, we’re dedicated to creating a diverse and welcoming team. If you’re passionate about this role and keen to learn and grow with us, we encourage you to apply— even if you don’t have everything thats listed just yet. Drop us your application, we’d love to hear from you! **🙌What’s In It For You** 💰€45,000 \- €55,000 ➕ incentive awards tied to your performance. ⏰We offer flexible working hours and trust you to work enough hours to do your job well, and at times that suit you and your team. 🏝 Annual Leave \- 34 days including public holidays (24 holiday days \+ 10 public holidays) 📚€1,200 learning budget each year to use on books, training courses and conferences. 🏥Private healthcare scheme 💰Pension scheme: the minimum contribution is 4% and Monzo matches any additional contributions that you make up to a maximum of 6% 💛Wellbeing benefits: financial education, women’s and men’s health support, mental health benefits, including coaching and counselling **🌈 The application journey has 4 key steps** * An introductory call with a Monzo Recruiter * An initial call with the hiring manager * A series of technical and behavioural interviews This process should take around 2\-3 weeks \- your schedule is really important to us, so we promise to be as flexible as possible! We have some guidelines on using Artificial Intelligence (AI) to ace an application and interview at Monzo. You can read them here. We’ll only close this role once we have enough applications for the next stage. Please submit your application as soon as possible to make sure you don’t miss out. If you're successful in applying for this role, we'll work with you to find a start date. In some cases, there might be a delay in when you can be released from your current role so that we can make sure things continue to run smoothly. We’ll be sure to communicate this with you and keep you updated. S1 **Equal opportunities for everyone** Diversity and inclusion are a priority for us and we’re making sure we have lots of support for all of our people to grow at Monzo. At Monzo, we’re embracing diversity by fostering an inclusive environment for all people to do the best work of their lives with us. This is integral to our mission of making money work for everyone. You can read more in our blog, 2026 Diversity and Inclusion Report and 2025 Gender Pay Gap Report. We’re an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to age, ethnicity, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, or veteran, neurodiversity or disability status. If you have a preferred name, please use it to apply. We don't need full or birth names at application stage 😊
